arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنْ شَيْخٍ، لَمْ يُسَمِّهِ، أَنَّ أَبَا بَكْرٍ، قَنَتَ فِي الْفَجْرِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Ibn Abi Dhi’b → A Shaykh he did not name
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Ibn Abi Dhi’b narrated to us from a Shaykh he did not name, that Abu Bakr performed Qunut in Fajr.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ أَبِي حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَعْقِلٍ، قَالَ: "قَنَتَ فِي الْفَجْرِ رَجُلَانِ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: عَلِيٌّ، وَأَبُو مُوسَى

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Abu Husayn → Abdullah ibn Ma’qil
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Abu Husayn, from Abdullah ibn Ma’qil, who said: “Two men from the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ performed Qunut in the Fajr prayer: Ali and Abu Musa.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ الرَّازِي، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَنَتَ فِي الْفَجْرِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Abu Ja’far AR-Razi → Ar-Rabi’ → Anas
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Abu Ja’far AR-Razi narrated to us, from Ar-Rabi’, from Anas: “That the Prophet ﷺ performed Qunut in the Fajr prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ عَوْفٍ، عَنْ أَبِي رَجَاءٍ الْعُطَارِدِيِّ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ يَمُدُّ بِضَبْعَيْهِ فِي قُنُوتِ صَلَاةِ الْغَدَاةِ، إِذَا كَانَ بِالْبَصْرَةِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Awf → Abu Raja’ al-Utaridi
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Awf, from Abu Raja’ al-Utaridi, who said: “I saw Ibn Abbas extending his arms [stretching them out] during the Qunut of the Morning prayer (Fajr) when he was in Basra.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَشْهَبِ جَعْفَرُ بْنُ حَيَّانَ، وَقُرَّةُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ، سَمِعَاهُ مِنْ أَبِي رَجَاءٍ الْعُطَارِدِيِّ، قَالَ: صَلَّى بِنَا ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ الْفَجْرَ، بِالْبَصْرَةِ فَقَنَتَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Abu al-Ashhab Ja’far ibn Hayyan & Qurrah ibn Khalid → Abu Raja’ al-Utaridi
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Abu al-Ashhab Ja’far ibn Hayyan and Qurrah ibn Khalid narrated to us, both hearing it from Abu Raja’ al-Utaridi, who said: “Ibn Abbas led us in the Fajr prayer in Basra and performed Qunut.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مِسْعَرٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ وَهْبٍ، قَالَ: رُبَّمَا قَنَتَ عُمَرُ فِي صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Mis’ar → Abdul Malik ibn Maysarah → Zayd ibn Wahb
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Mis’ar narrated to us, from Abdul Malik ibn Maysarah, from Zayd ibn Wahb, who said: “Umar would sometimes perform Qunut in the Fajr prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ زُبَيْدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، قَالَ: الْقُنُوتُ سُنَّةٌ مَاضِيَةٌ

english.narrator: Sharik → Zubayd → Ibn Abi Layla
english.text: Sharik narrated to us, from Zubayd, from Ibn Abi Layla, who said: “Qunut is an established Sunnah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ زُبَيْدِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ الْيَامِيِّ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ ابْنَ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ الْقُنُوتِ فِي الْفَجْرِ؟ فَقَالَ: سُنَّةٌ مَاضِيَةٌ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Zubayd ibn al-Harith al-Yami
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Zubayd ibn al-Harith al-Yami, who said: I asked Ibn Abi Layla about Qunut in Fajr? He said: “It is an established Sunnah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: الْقُنُوتُ فِي الْفَجْرِ هُنَيْهَةٌ، أَوْ سَاعَةٌ، أَوْ كَلِمَةٌ تُشْبِهُهَا

english.narrator: Waki’ → Yazid ibn Harun → Ibn Sirin
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, from Ibn Sirin, that he said: “Qunut in Fajr is a brief moment, or a short time, or a word similar to that.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مُحَارِبِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ الْبَرَاءِ، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقْنُتُ فِي الْفَجْرِ

english.narrator: Abu Mahdi → Sufyan → Muharib ibn Ubayd ibn al-Bara’ → Al-Bara’
english.text: Abu Mahdi narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Muharib ibn Ubayd ibn al-Bara’, from al-Bara’, “That he used to perform Qunut in Fajr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ وَكِيعًا، يَقُولُ: سَمِعْتُ سُفْيَانَ، يَقُولُ: مَنْ قَنَتَ فَحَسَنٌ، وَمَنْ لَمْ يَقْنُتْ فَحَسَنٌ، وَمَنْ قَنَتَ فَإِنَّمَا الْقُنُوتُ عَلَى الْإِمَامِ، وَلَيْسَ عَلَى مَنْ وَرَاءَهُ قُنُوتٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki’ → Sufyan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: I heard Waki’ say: I heard Sufyan say: “Whoever performs Qunut, it is good; and whoever does not perform Qunut, it is good. And whoever performs Qunut, indeed the Qunut is upon the Imam, and there is no Qunut upon those behind him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ الْعَوَّامِ بْنِ حَمْزَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عُثْمَانَ عَنْ الْقُنُوتِ، فَقَالَ: بَعْدَ الرُّكُوعِ، فَقُلْتُ: عَمَّنْ؟ فَقَالَ: عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، وَعُثْمَانَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya ibn Sa’id → Al-Awwam ibn Hamzah → Abu Uthman
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yahya ibn Sa’id narrated to us, from al-Awwam ibn Hamzah, who said: I asked Abu Uthman about Qunut. He said: “After the bowing (Ruku’).” I said: “From whom?” He said: “From Abu Bakr and Uthman.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ عَوْفٍ، عَنْ أَبِي رَجَاءٍ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ مَعَ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، فِي مَسْجِدِ الْبَصْرَةِ، صَلَاةَ الْغَدَاةِ، فَقَنَتَ بِنَا قَبْلَ الرُّكُوعِ

english.narrator: Marwan ibn Mu’awiyah → Awf → Abu Raja’
english.text: Marwan ibn Mu’awiyah narrated to us, from Awf, from Abu Raja’, who said: “I prayed the Morning prayer (Fajr) with Ibn Abbas in the Mosque of Basra, and he performed Qunut with us before the bowing (Ruku’).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ عَوْفٍ، قَالَ: ذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ لِأَبِي الْمِنْهَالِ، فَحَدَّثَنِي عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، بِمِثْلِهِ

english.narrator: Marwan ibn Mu’awiyah → Awf → Abu al-Minhal → Abu al-Aliyah → Ibn Abbas
english.text: Marwan ibn Mu’awiyah narrated to us, from Awf, who said: I mentioned that to Abu al-Minhal, so he narrated to me from Abu al-Aliyah, from Ibn Abbas, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى بْنِ حِبَّانَ، قَالَ: قَنَتَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَرْبَعِينَ صَبَاحًا فِي صَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ بَعْدَ الرُّكُوعِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Yahya ibn Sa’id → Muhammad ibn Yahya ibn Hibban
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Yahya ibn Sa’id, from Muhammad ibn Yahya ibn Hibban, who said: “The Prophet ﷺ performed Qunut for forty mornings in the Subh (Fajr) prayer after the bowing (Ruku’).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَيَّاشٍ الْعَامِرِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ مُغَفَّلٍ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ، وَعَلِيًّا، وَأَبَا مُوسَى، قَنَتُوا فِي الْفَجْرِ قَبْلَ الرُّكُوعِ

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn Fudayl → Hajjaj → Ayyash al-Amiri → Ibn Mughaffal
english.text: Muhammad ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Hajjaj, from Ayyash al-Amiri, from Ibn Mughaffal: “That Umar, Ali, and Abu Musa performed Qunut in Fajr before the bowing (Ruku’).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْجَهْمِ، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقْنُتُ قَبْلَ الرَّكْعَةِ

english.narrator: Ibn Fudayl → Mutarrif → Abu al-Jahm → Al-Bara’
english.text: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Mutarrif, from Abu al-Jahm, from al-Bara’: “That he used to perform Qunut before the bowing (Rak’ah/Ruku’).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ وَهْبٍ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ قَنَتَ فِي صَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ قَبْلَ الرُّكُوعِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad → Zayd ibn Wahb
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad informed us, saying: Zayd ibn Wahb narrated to us: “That Umar ibn al-Khattab performed Qunut in the Subh prayer before the bowing (Ruku’).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو عُثْمَانَ النَّهْدِيُّ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ خَلْفَ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ، صَلَاةَ الصُّبْحِ فَقَنَتَ قَبْلَ الرُّكُوعِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Ali ibn Zayd → Abu Uthman an-Nahdi
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Ali ibn Zayd informed us, saying: Abu Uthman an-Nahdi informed us, saying: “I prayed the Subh prayer behind Umar ibn al-Khattab, and he performed Qunut before the bowing (Ruku’).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا عَطَاءُ بْنُ السَّائِبِ، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ السُّلَمِيِّ، أَنَّ عَلِيًّا كَانَ يَقْنُتُ فِي صَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ قَبْلَ الرُّكُوعِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Ata’ ibn as-Sa’ib → Abu Abdul Rahman as-Sulami
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Ata’ ibn as-Sa’ib informed us, from Abu Abdul Rahman as-Sulami: “That Ali used to perform Qunut in the Subh prayer before the bowing (Ruku’).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ خَلْفَ عُمَرَ الْغَدَاةَ، قَالَ: فَقَنَتَ فِيهَا قَبْلَ الرُّكُوعِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Ibn Abi Layla → Ata’ → Ubayd ibn Umayr
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Ibn Abi Layla informed us, from Ata’, from Ubayd ibn Umayr, who said: “I prayed the Morning prayer (Ghadah) behind Umar.” He said: “And he performed Qunut in it before the bowing (Ruku’).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عُمَرَ، بِمِثْلِهِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Ibn Abi Layla → Ata’ → Ubayd ibn Umayr → Umar
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Ibn Abi Layla narrated to us, from Ata’, from Ubayd ibn Umayr, from Umar, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ نُسَيْرِ بْنِ ذُعْلُوقٍ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ خَلْفَ رَبِيعِ بْنِ خَيْثَمٍ، فَقَنَتَ قَبْلَ الرَّكْعَةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Nusayr ibn Dhu’luq
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Nusayr ibn Dhu’luq, who said: “I prayed behind Rabi’ ibn Khaytham, and he performed Qunut before the bowing (Rak’ah).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنِ النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ خَلْفَ عَبِيدَةُ الْفَجْرَ فَقَنَتَ قَبْلَ الرَّكْعَةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → An-Nu’man ibn Qays
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from an-Nu’man ibn Qays, who said: “I prayed Fajr behind Abidah, and he performed Qunut before the bowing (Rak’ah).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، عَنْ زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي فَرْوَةَ، قَالَ: ابْنُ أَبِي لَيْلَى، يَقْنُتُ فِي الْفَجْرِ قَبْلَ الرَّكْعَةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Husayn ibn Ali → Za’idah → Abu Farwah
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Husayn ibn Ali narrated to us, from Za’idah, from Abu Farwah, who said: “Ibn Abi Layla performs Qunut in Fajr before the bowing (Rak’ah).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ وُهَيْبٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: كَانَ يَدْعُو بِدُعَاءٍ كَثِيرٍ فِي صَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ قَبْلَ الرُّكُوعِ

english.narrator: Ahmad ibn Ishaq → Wuhayb → Ibn Tawus → His Father
english.text: Ahmad ibn Ishaq narrated to us, from Wuhayb, from Ibn Tawus, from his father, who said: “He used to supplicate with much supplication in the Subh prayer before the bowing (Ruku’).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ خَلْفَ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ الْغَدَاةَ، فَقَالَ فِي قُنُوتِهِ: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَسْتَعِينُكَ وَنَسْتَغْفِرُكَ، وَنُثْنِي عَلَيْكَ الْخَيْرَ، وَلَا نَكْفُرُكَ، وَنَخْلَعُ، وَنَتْرُكُ مَنْ يَفْجُرُكَ، اللَّهُمَّ إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ، وَلَكَ نُصَلِّي، وَنَسْجُدُ وَإِلَيْكَ نَسْعَى وَنَحْفِدُ، وَنَرْجُو رَحْمَتَكَ، وَنَخْشَى عَذَابَكَ، إِنَّ عَذَابَكَ بِالْكُفَّارِ مُلْحِقٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Ibn Abi Layla → Ata’ → Ubayd ibn Umayr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Ibn Abi Layla informed us, from Ata’, from Ubayd ibn Umayr, who said: I prayed the Morning prayer (Ghadah) behind Umar ibn al-Khattab, and he said in his Qunut: “O Allah, we seek Your help and we seek Your forgiveness, and we praise You with good, and we do not disbelieve in You, and we cast off and abandon those who disobey You. O Allah, You alone we worship, and to You we pray and prostrate, and to You we strive and hasten. We hope for Your mercy and we fear Your punishment. Indeed, Your punishment will overtake the disbelievers.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا حُصَيْنٌ، عَنْ زِرٍّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبْزَى، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ صَلَّى خَلْفَ عُمَرَ فَصَنَعَ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Husayn → Zirr → Sa’id ibn Abdul Rahman ibn Abza → His Father
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Husayn informed us, from Zirr, from Sa’id ibn Abdul Rahman ibn Abza, from his father, that he prayed behind Umar and he did the same.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ حَبِيبِ بْنِ أَبِي ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ سُوَيْدٍ الْكَاهِلِيِّ، أَنَّ عَلِيًّا قَنَتَ فِي الْفَجْرِ بِهَاتَيْنِ السُّورَتَيْنِ: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَسْتَعِينُكَ وَنَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَنُثْنِي عَلَيْكَ الْخَيْرَ، وَلَا نَكْفُرُ، وَنَخْلَعُ وَنَتْرُكُ مَنْ يَفْجُرُكَ، اللَّهُمَّ إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَلَكَ نُصَلِّي وَنَسْجُدُ، وَإِلَيْكَ نَسْعَى وَنَحْفِدُ نَرْجُو رَحْمَتَكَ، وَنَخْشَى عَذَابَكَ، إِنَّ عَذَابَكَ الْجِدَّ بِالْكُفَّارِ مُلْحِقٌ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Habib ibn Abi Thabit → Abdul Malik ibn Suwayd al-Kahili
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Habib ibn Abi Thabit, from Abdul Malik ibn Suwayd al-Kahili, that Ali performed Qunut in Fajr with these two Surahs: “O Allah, we seek Your help and we seek Your forgiveness, and we praise You with good, and we do not disbelieve, and we cast off and abandon those who disobey You. O Allah, You alone we worship, and to You we pray and prostrate, and to You we strive and hasten. We hope for Your mercy and we fear Your punishment. Indeed, Your severe punishment will overtake the disbelievers.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ بُرْقَانَ، عَنْ مَيْمُونِ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ، فِي قِرَاءَةِ أُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَسْتَعِينُكَ وَنَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَنُثْنِي عَلَيْكَ، وَلَا نَكْفُرُكَ، وَنَخْلَعُ، وَنَتْرُكُ مَنْ يَفْجُرُكَ، اللَّهُمَّ إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ، وَلَكَ نُصَلِّي وَنَسْجُدُ، وَإِلَيْكَ نَسْعَى وَنَرْجُو رَحْمَتَكَ، وَنَخْشَى عَذَابَكَ، إِنَّ عَذَابَكَ بِالْكُفَّارِ مُلْحِقٌ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Ja’far ibn Burqan → Maymun ibn Mihran
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Ja’far ibn Burqan informed us, from Maymun ibn Mihran, concerning the recitation of Ubayy ibn Ka’b: “O Allah, we seek Your help and we seek Your forgiveness, and we praise You, and we do not disbelieve in You, and we cast off and abandon those who disobey You. O Allah, You alone we worship, and to You we pray and prostrate, and to You we strive, and we hope for Your mercy and we fear Your punishment. Indeed, Your punishment will overtake the disbelievers.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ نُمَيْرٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عُمَرَ، يَقْنُتُ فِي الْفَجْرِ يَقُولُ: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَسْتَعِينُكَ وَنُؤْمِنُ بِكَ، وَنَتَوَكَّلُ عَلَيْكَ وَنُثْنِي عَلَيْكَ الْخَيْرَ كُلَّهُ، وَلَا نَكْفُرُ ثُمَّ قَرَأَ: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ اللَّهُمَّ إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ، وَلَكَ نُصَلِّي وَنَسْجُدُ، وَإِلَيْكَ نَسْعَى وَنَحْفِدُ، نَرْجُو رَحْمَتَكَ، وَنَخْشَى عَذَابَكَ، إِنَّ عَذَابَكَ الْجِدَّ بِالْكُفَّارِ مُلْحِقٌ، اللَّهُمَّ عَذِّبْ كَفَرَةَ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ الَّذِينَ يَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِكَ

english.narrator: Hafs ibn Ghiyath → Ibn Jurayj → Ata’ → Ubayd ibn Numayr
english.text: Hafs ibn Ghiyath narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from Ata’, from Ubayd ibn Numayr, who said: I heard Umar performing Qunut in Fajr saying: “In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. O Allah, we seek Your help and we believe in You, and we rely upon You, and we praise You with all good, and we do not disbelieve.” Then he recited: “In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. O Allah, You alone we worship, and to You we pray and prostrate, and to You we strive and hasten. We hope for Your mercy and we fear Your punishment. Indeed, Your severe punishment will overtake the disbelievers. O Allah, punish the disbelievers of the People of the Book who obstruct Your path.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا حُصَيْنٌ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ الْغَدَاةَ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ، وَصَلَّى خَلْفِي عُثْمَانُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ، قَالَ: فَقَنَتُّ فِي صَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ، قَالَ: فَلَمَّا قَضَيْتُ صَلَاتِي، قَالَ لِي: مَا قُلْتَ فِي قُنُوتِكَ؟ قَالَ: فَقُلْتُ: ذَكَرْتُ هَؤُلَاءِ الْكَلِمَاتِ: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَسْتَعِينُكَ وَنَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَنُثْنِي عَلَيْكَ الْخَيْرَ كُلَّهُ، وَلَا نَكْفُرُكَ، وَنَخْلَعُ وَنَتْرُكُ مَنْ يَفْجُرُكَ، اللَّهُمَّ إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَلَكَ نُصَلِّي وَنَسْجُدُ، وَإِلَيْكَ نَسْعَى وَنَحْفِدُ نَرْجُو رَحْمَتَكَ، وَنَخْشَى عَذَابَكَ، إِنَّ عَذَابَكَ الْجِدَّ بِالْكُفَّارِ مُلْحِقٌ، فَقَالَ عُثْمَانُ: كَذَا كَانَ يَصْنَعُ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ، وَعُثْمَانُ بْنُ عَفَّانَ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Husayn
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Husayn informed us, saying: I prayed the Morning prayer (Ghadah) one day, and Uthman ibn Ziyad prayed behind me. He said: So I performed Qunut in the Subh prayer. He said: When I finished my prayer, he said to me: “What did you say in your Qunut?” He said: So I said: I recited these words: “O Allah, we seek Your help and we seek Your forgiveness, and we praise You with all good, and we do not disbelieve in You, and we cast off and abandon those who disobey You. O Allah, You alone we worship, and to You we pray and prostrate, and to You we strive and hasten. We hope for Your mercy and we fear Your punishment. Indeed, Your severe punishment will overtake the disbelievers.” Uthman then said: “This is how Umar ibn al-Khattab and Uthman ibn Affan used to do.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعُ بْنُ الْجَرَّاحِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مُخَارِقٍ، عَنْ طَارِقِ بْنِ شِهَابٍ، أَنَّهُ صَلَّى خَلْفَ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ الْفَجْرَ، فَلَمَّا فَرَغَ مِنَ الْقِرَاءَةِ كَبَّرَ، ثُمَّ قَنَتَ، ثُمَّ كَبَّرَ، ثُمَّ رَكَعَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki’ ibn al-Jarrah → Sufyan → Mukhariq → Tariq ibn Shihab
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki’ ibn al-Jarrah narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Mukhariq, from Tariq ibn Shihab: “That he prayed Fajr behind Umar ibn al-Khattab. When he finished the recitation, he said Takbir, then performed Qunut, then said Takbir, then bowed (Ruku’).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ السُّلَمِيِّ، أَنَّ عَلِيًّا، كَبَّرَ حِينَ قَنَتَ فِي الْفَجْرِ، وَكَبَّرَ حِينَ رَكَعَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Abdul A’la → Abu Abdul Rahman as-Sulami
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Abdul A’la, from Abu Abdul Rahman as-Sulami: “That Ali said Takbir when he performed Qunut in Fajr, and said Takbir when he bowed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْجَهْمِ، قَالَ: كَانَ الْبَرَاءُ يُكَبِّرُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَقْنُتَ

english.narrator: Ibn Fudayl → Mutarrif → Abu al-Jahm
english.text: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Mutarrif, from Abu al-Jahm, who said: “Al-Bara’ used to say Takbir before he performed Qunut.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْجَهْمِ، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ، أَنَّهُ قَنَتَ فِي الْفَجْرِ فَكَبَّرَ حِينَ فَرَغَ مِنَ الْقِرَاءَةِ، وَكَبَّرَ حِينَ رَكَعَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Mutarrif → Abu al-Jahm → Al-Bara’
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Mutarrif, from Abu al-Jahm, from al-Bara’: “That he performed Qunut in Fajr, so he said Takbir when he finished the recitation, and he said Takbir when he bowed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ، عَنْ أَبِي سِنَانٍ، عَنْ مَاهَانَ قَالَ: كَانَ يُكَبِّرُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَقْنُتَ فِي صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ حِينَ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَرْكَعَ

english.narrator: Humayd ibn Abdul Rahman → Hasan → Abu Sinan → Mahan
english.text: Humayd ibn Abdul Rahman narrated to us, from Hasan, from Abu Sinan, from Mahan, who said: “He used to say Takbir before performing Qunut in the Fajr prayer when he wanted to bow.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُكَبِّرُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَقْنُتَ فِي صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ، وَحِينَ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَرْكَعَ

english.narrator: Humayd ibn Abdul Rahman → Hasan → Abdul A’la → Abu Abdul Rahman
english.text: Humayd ibn Abdul Rahman narrated to us, from Hasan, from Abdul A’la, from Abu Abdul Rahman: “That he used to say Takbir before performing Qunut in the Fajr prayer, and when he wanted to bow.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ زُهَيْرٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي إِسْحَاقَ: تُكَبِّرُ أَنْتَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَقْنُتَ فِي صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ

english.narrator: Humayd ibn Zuhayr → Abu Ishaq
english.text: Humayd ibn Zuhayr narrated to us, saying: I said to Abu Ishaq: “Do you say Takbir before you perform Qunut in the Fajr prayer?” He said: “Yes.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا نَضْرُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَفْتَتِحُ الْقُنُوتَ بِالتَّكْبِيرِ

english.narrator: Nadr ibn Isma’il → Ibn Abi Layla → Abu Ishaq → Al-Harith → Ali
english.text: Nadr ibn Isma’il narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Layla, from Abu Ishaq, from al-Harith, from Ali: “That he used to open the Qunut with Takbir.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ، قَالَ: كَانَ عُمَرُ يَقْنُتُ بِنَا بَعْدَ الرُّكُوعِ، وَيَرْفَعُ يَدَيْهِ حَتَّى يَبْدُوَ ضَبْعَاهُ، وَيُسْمَعَ صَوْتُهُ مِنْ وَرَاءِ الْمَسْجِدِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya ibn Sa’id → Ja’far ibn Maymun → Abu Uthman
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yahya ibn Sa’id narrated to us, from Ja’far ibn Maymun, from Abu Uthman, who said: “Umar used to perform Qunut with us after the bowing (Ruku’), and he would raise his hands until his armpits appeared, and his voice could be heard from the back of the mosque.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ، صَاحِبِ الْأَنْمَاطِ، عَنْ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ، رَفَعَ يَدَيْهِ فِي قُنُوتِ الْفَجْرِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Ja’far (The owner of rugs) → Abu Uthman
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Ja’far (the owner of rugs), from Abu Uthman: “That Umar raised his hands in the Qunut of Fajr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَوْفٍ، عَنْ خَلَّاسِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو الْهَجَرِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّهُ صَلَّى، فَقَنَتَ بِهِمْ فِي الْفَجْرِ بِالْبَصْرَةِ، فَرَفَعَ يَدَيْهِ حَتَّى مَدَّ ضَبْعَيْهِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Awf → Khallas ibn Amr al-Hajari → Ibn Abbas
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Awf, from Khallas ibn Amr al-Hajari, from Ibn Abbas: “That he prayed and performed Qunut with them in Fajr in Basra, so he raised his hands until he stretched out his upper arms.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ عَوْفٍ، عَنْ أَبِي رَجَاءٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ يَمُدُّ بِضَبْعَيْهِ فِي قُنُوتِ صَلَاةِ الْغَدَاةِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Awf → Abu Raja’
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Awf, from Abu Raja’, who said: “I saw Ibn Abbas extending his arms [stretching them out] in the Qunut of the Morning prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، عَنْ زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي فَرْوَةَ، قَالَ: كَانَ ابْنُ أَبِي لَيْلَى يَدْعُو بِإِصْبَعٍ وَاحِدَةٍ يَقُولُ فِي قُنُوتِ الْفَجْرِ

english.narrator: Husayn ibn Ali → Za’idah → Abu Farwah
english.text: Husayn ibn Ali narrated to us, from Za’idah, from Abu Farwah, who said: “Ibn Abi Layla used to supplicate with one finger, meaning in the Qunut of Fajr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: لَمَّا رَفَعَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ رَأْسَهُ مِنْ صَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ، قَالَ: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْجِ الْوَلِيدَ بْنَ الْوَلِيدِ، وَسَلَمَةَ بْنَ هِشَامٍ، وَعَيَّاشَ بْنَ أَبِي رَبِيعَةَ، وَالْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ بِمَكَّةَ، اللَّهُمَّ اشْدُدْ وَطْأَتَكَ عَلَى مُضَرَ، وَاجْعَلْهَا عَلَيْهِمْ سِنِينَ كَسِنِي يُوسُفَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Uyaynah → Az-Zuhri → Sa’id → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Uyaynah narrated to us, from az-Zuhri, from Sa’id, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: When the Prophet ﷺ raised his head from the Subh prayer, he said: “O Allah, save al-Walid ibn al-Walid, and Salamah ibn Hisham, and Ayyash ibn Abi Rabi’ah, and the weak and oppressed in Makkah. O Allah, tighten Your grip on Mudar, and make it upon them years [of famine] like the years of Joseph.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي يُحَنَّسَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، قَالَ: قَنَتَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَقَالَ: اللَّهُمَّ الْعَنْ رِعْلًا، وَذَكْوَانَ، وَعَضَلًا، وَعُصَيَّةَ عَصَتِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ، وَالْعَنْ أَبَا الْأَعْوَرِ السُّلَمِيَّ

english.narrator: Ya’qub ibn Ibrahim → Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad → Ibn Abi Yuhannas → Sa’id ibn Zayd
english.text: Ya’qub ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, from Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad, from Ibn Abi Yuhannas, from Sa’id ibn Zayd, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed Qunut and said: “O Allah, curse Ri’l, Dhakwan, and Adal, and Usayyah who disobeyed Allah and His Messenger, and curse Abu al-A’war as-Sulami.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، أَنَّ أَبَا عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ السُّلَمِيَّ، قَنَتَ فِي الْفَجْرِ يَدْعُو عَلَى فَطَّةَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Abdul A’la
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Abdul A’la: “That Abu Abdul Rahman as-Sulami performed Qunut in Fajr supplicating against Fattah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ قَيْسٍ، عَمَّنْ سَمِعَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، يَقُولُ: لَا يُسَمَّى الرِّجَالُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Amr ibn Qays → Heard Ibrahim
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Amr ibn Qays, from one who heard Ibrahim say: “Men are not to be named in the prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا حُصَيْنٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَعْقِلٍ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ مَعَ عَلِيٍّ صَلَاةَ الْغَدَاةِ، قَالَ: فَقَنَتَ، فَقَالَ فِي قُنُوتِهِ: اللَّهُمَّ عَلَيْكَ بِمُعَاوِيَةَ وَأَشْيَاعِهِ، وَعَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ وَأَشْيَاعِهِ، وَأَبِي السُّلَمِيِّ وَأَشْيَاعِهِ، وَعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ وَأَشْيَاعِهِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Husayn → Abdul Rahman ibn Ma’qil
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Husayn informed us, saying: Abdul Rahman ibn Ma’qil narrated to us, saying: I prayed the Morning prayer (Ghadah) with Ali. He said: He performed Qunut and said in his Qunut: “O Allah, deal with Mu’awiyah and his partisans, and Amr ibn al-Aas and his partisans, and Abu as-Sulami and his partisans, and Abdullah ibn Qays and his partisans.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى بْنِ حِبَّانَ، قَالَ: مَكَثَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَرْبَعِينَ صَبَاحًا، يَقْنُتُ فِي صَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ بَعْدَ الرُّكُوعِ، وَكَانَ يَقُولُ فِي قُنُوتِهِ: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْجِ الْوَلِيدَ بْنَ الْوَلِيدِ، وَعَيَّاشَ بْنَ أَبِي رَبِيعَةَ، وَالْعَاصَ بْنَ هِشَامٍ، وَالْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ بِمَكَّةَ الَّذِينَ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ حِيلَةً، وَلَا يَهْتَدُونَ سَبِيلًا

english.narrator: Hushaym → Yahya ibn Sa’id → Muhammad ibn Yahya ibn Hibban
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Yahya ibn Sa’id, who said: Muhammad ibn Yahya ibn Hibban narrated to us, saying: The Prophet ﷺ remained for forty mornings performing Qunut in the Subh prayer after the bowing (Ruku’), and he used to say in his Qunut: “O Allah, save al-Walid ibn al-Walid, and Ayyash ibn Abi Rabi’ah, and al-Aas ibn Hisham, and the weak and oppressed believers in Makkah who cannot find a device (for escape) nor are they guided to a path.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ بْنِ أَبِي أَنَسٍ، عَنْ حَنْظَلَةَ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ الْأَسْلَمِيِّ، عَنْ خُفَافِ بْنِ إِيمَاءِ بْنِ رَحَضَةَ الْغِفَارِيِّ، قَالَ: صَلَّى بِنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ الْفَجْرَ فَلَمَّا رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ مِنَ الرَّكْعَةِ الْآخِرَةِ، قَالَ: لَعَنَ اللَّهُ لِحْيَانًا، وَرِعْلًا، وَذَكْوانًا، وَعُصَيَّةَ عَصَتِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ، أَسْلَمُ سَالَمَهَا اللَّهُ، غِفَارٌ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهَا، ثُمَّ خَرَّ سَاجِدًا، فَلَمَّا قَضَى الصَّلَاةَ، أَقْبَلَ عَلَى النَّاسِ بِوَجْهِهِ، فَقَالَ: أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنِّي أَنَا لَسْتُ قُلْتُ هَذَا، وَلَكِنَّ اللَّهَ قَالَهُ

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Muhammad ibn Ishaq → Imran ibn Abi Anas → Hanzalah ibn Ali al-Aslami → Khufaf ibn Ima’ ibn Rahadah al-Ghifari
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, saying: Muhammad ibn Ishaq informed us, from Imran ibn Abi Anas, from Hanzalah ibn Ali al-Aslami, from Khufaf ibn Ima’ ibn Rahadah al-Ghifari, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ led us in the Fajr prayer. When he raised his head from the last Rak’ah, he said: “May Allah curse Lihyan, Ri’l, Dhakwan, and Usayyah who disobeyed Allah and His Messenger. Aslam, may Allah grant it peace (salamaha Allah). Ghifar, may Allah forgive it (ghafara Allah laha).” Then he fell down in prostration. When he finished the prayer, he turned his face to the people and said: “O people, indeed I did not say this (of my own accord), but Allah said it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِذَا نَسِيَ الْقُنُوتَ فِي الْفَجْرِ، فَعَلَيْهِ سَجْدَتَا السَّهْوِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, from Yunus, from al-Hasan, who said: “If one forgets the Qunut in Fajr, he must perform the two prostrations of forgetfulness (Sujud as-Sahw).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، قَالَ: سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ سَهَا فَقَنَتَ، فَقَالَ: هَذَا سَهَا فَأَصَابَ

english.narrator: Sharik → Ibn Abi Layla
english.text: Sharik narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Layla, who said: He was asked about a man who was forgetful and (so he) performed Qunut. He said: “This one was forgetful but he did the right thing.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ عُقْبَةَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: مَنْ رَأَى الْقُنُوتَ فَلَمْ يَقْنُتْ، فَعَلَيْهِ سَجْدَتَا السَّهْوِ

english.narrator: Al-Walid ibn Uqbah → Sufyan → Ibn Jurayj → Ata’
english.text: Al-Walid ibn Uqbah narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from Ata’, who said: “Whoever holds the view of (performing) Qunut but does not perform Qunut, he must perform the two prostrations of forgetfulness.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، وَوَكِيعٌ، وَغُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ بْنِ عَازِبٍ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، قَنَتَ فِي الصُّبْحِ، وَالْمَغْرِبِ، قَالَ: فَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ: أَهُوَ كَأَصْحَابِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، إِنَّمَا هُوَ صَاحِبُ امْرِئٍ، وَلَمْ يَذْكُرْ وَكِيعٌ، قَوْلَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abdullah ibn Idris & Waki’ & Ghundar → Shu’bah → Amr ibn Murrah → Ibn Abi Layla → Al-Bara’ ibn Azib
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abdullah ibn Idris, Waki’, and Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Amr ibn Murrah, from Ibn Abi Layla, from al-Bara’ ibn Azib: “That the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed Qunut in the Subh and Maghrib prayers.” He said: Ibrahim then said: “Is he like the companions of Abdullah? He is only a companion of a man.” And Waki’ did not mention the statement of Ibrahim.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ مَعْقِلٍ، قَالَ: قَنَتَ عَلِيٌّ فِي الْمَغْرِبِ

english.narrator: Sharik → Husayn → Abdul Rahman ibn Ma’qil
english.text: Sharik narrated to us, from Husayn, from Abdul Rahman ibn Ma’qil, who said: “Ali performed Qunut in Maghrib.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، قَالَ: صَلَاتَانِ كَانَ يَقْنُتُ فِيهِمَا الْمَغْرِبُ، وَالْفَجْرُ

english.narrator: Ibn Ulayyah → Khalid → Abu Qilabah → Anas
english.text: Ibn Ulayyah narrated to us, from Khalid, from Abu Qilabah, from Anas, who said: “Two prayers in which he used to perform Qunut were Maghrib and Fajr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ مُغَفَّلٍ، قَالَ: قَنَتَ عَلِيٌّ فِي الْمَغْرِبِ

english.narrator: Abu Mu’awiyah → Al-A’mash → Abdullah ibn Khalid → Ibn Mughaffal
english.text: Abu Mu’awiyah narrated to us, from al-A’mash, from Abdullah ibn Khalid, from Ibn Mughaffal, who said: “Ali performed Qunut in Maghrib.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ الثُّمَالِيِّ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ، عَنِ الْقُنُوتِ، فَقَالَ: كُلُّ صَلَاةٍ يُجْهَرُ فِيهَا، فَفِيهَا الْقُنُوتُ

english.narrator: Sharik → Thabit ath-Thumali → Abu Ja’far
english.text: Sharik narrated to us, from Thabit ath-Thumali, who said: I asked Abu Ja’far about Qunut. He said: “Every prayer in which recitation is made aloud, there is Qunut in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنِ الْمِنْهَالِ، عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ، قَالَ: رَأَى عَبْدُ اللَّهِ رَجُلًا يُصَلِّي صَافًّا بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ، فَقَالَ: لَوْ رَاوَحَ هَذَا بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ، كَانَ أَفْضَلَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs ibn Ghiyath → Al-A’mash → Al-Minhal → Abu Ubaydah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs ibn Ghiyath narrated to us, from al-A’mash, from al-Minhal, from Abu Ubaydah, who said: Abdullah saw a man praying with his feet aligned [rigidly together], so he said: “If this one had alternated [rested/shifted weight] between his feet, it would have been better.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَيْسَرَةَ الْهُذَلِيِّ، عَنِ الْمِنْهَالِ، عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ، قَالَ: خَرَجَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ مِنْ دَارِهِ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ، وَإِذَا رَجُلٌ يُصَلِّي صَافًّا بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ، فَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: أَمَّا هَذَا فَقَدْ أَخْطَأَ السُّنَّةَ، وَلَوْ رَاوَحَ بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ كَانَ أَحَبَّ إِلَيَّ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Maysarah al-Hudhali → Al-Minhal → Abu Ubaydah
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Maysarah al-Hudhali, from al-Minhal, from Abu Ubaydah, who said: Abdullah went out from his house to the mosque, and there was a man praying with his feet aligned [rigidly]. Abdullah said: “As for this one, he has missed the Sunnah. If he had alternated [shifted weight] between his feet, it would have been more beloved to me.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ عُيَيْنَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ مَعَ أَبِي فِي الْمَسْجِدِ، فَرَأَى رَجُلًا صَافًّا بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ، فَقَالَ: أَلْزِقْ إِحْدَاهُمَا بِالْأُخْرَى، لَقَدْ رَأَيْتُ فِي هَذَا الْمَسْجِدِ ثَمَانِيَةَ عَشَرَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، مَا رَأَيْتُ أَحَدًا مِنْهُمْ فَعَلَ هَذَا قَطُّ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Uyaynah ibn Abdul Rahman → His Father
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Uyaynah ibn Abdul Rahman, who said: I was with my father in the mosque, and he saw a man aligning his feet [rigidly together]. He said [sarcastically/critically]: “Stick one of them to the other [completely then]! I have seen in this mosque eighteen of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ, and I never saw anyone of them doing this ever.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَمْرَو بْنَ مَيْمُونٍ يُرَاوِحُ بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Sharik → Abu Ishaq
english.text: Sharik narrated to us, from Abu Ishaq, who said: “I saw Amr ibn Maymun alternating [shifting weight] between his feet in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَمْرَو بْنَ مَيْمُونٍ، يُرَاوِحُ بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ، يَضَعُ هَذِهِ عَلَى هَذِهِ وَهَذِهِ عَلَى هَذِهِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Isma’il ibn Abi Khalid
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Isma’il ibn Abi Khalid, who said: “I saw Amr ibn Maymun alternating between his feet, placing [weight] on this one and on this one.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ يُوسُفَ بْنِ عَبْدَةَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ سِيرِينَ يُصَلِّي وَهُوَ هَكَذَا، يَعْنِي يُقَدِّمُ رِجْلًا، وَيُؤَخِّرُ أُخْرَى

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn Abi Adi → Yusuf ibn Abdah
english.text: Muhammad ibn Abi Adi narrated to us, from Yusuf ibn Abdah, who said: “I saw Ibn Sirin praying while he was like this,” meaning he would put one foot forward and the other back.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ يُرَاوِحُ بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Hisham
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, from Hisham, who said: “Ibn Sirin used to alternate between his feet in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ رَاشِدٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ مَكْحُولًا يَتَّكِئُ عَلَى قَدَمَيْهِ عَلَى هَذِهِ مَرَّةً، وَعَلَى هَذِهِ مَرَّةً فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Ma’n ibn Isa → Abdullah ibn Rashid
english.text: Ma’n ibn Isa narrated to us, from Abdullah ibn Rashid, who said: “I saw Makhul leaning on his feet, on this one once and on this one once in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنْ خَالِدِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ سَالِمًا لَا يَصُفُّ قَدَمَيْهِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ، وَيُحَرِّكُهَا وَهُوَ يُصَلِّي

english.narrator: Ma’n ibn Isa → Khalid ibn Abi Bakr
english.text: Ma’n ibn Isa narrated to us, from Khalid ibn Abi Bakr, who said: “I saw Salim not aligning his feet [rigidly] in prayer, and he would move them while praying.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، أَنَّ ابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ، كَانَ يَصُفُّ بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abdah ibn Sulayman → Hisham ibn Urwah → A Man
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abdah ibn Sulayman narrated to us, from Hisham ibn Urwah, from a man: “That Ibn az-Zubayr used to align his feet in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي مَنْ، رَأَى ابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ، يُصَلِّي قَدْ صَفَّ بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ، وَأَلْزَقَ إِحْدَاهُمَا بِالْأُخْرَى

english.narrator: Waki’ → Hisham ibn Urwah
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Hisham ibn Urwah, who said: I was informed by someone who saw Ibn az-Zubayr praying, having aligned his feet and stuck one of them to the other.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ مُغَفَّلٍ يُصَلِّي صَافًّا بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Husayn
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Husayn, who said: “I saw Ibn Mughaffal praying, aligning his feet.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ مُسْلِمَ بْنَ يَسَارٍ يُصَلِّي كَأَنَّهُ وِدٌّ لَا يَتَرَوَّحُ عَلَى رِجْلٍ مَرَّةً، وَعَلَى رِجْلٍ مَرَّةً

english.narrator: Mu’adh ibn Mu’adh → Ibn Awn
english.text: Mu’adh ibn Mu’adh narrated to us, from Ibn Awn, who said: “I saw Muslim ibn Yasar praying as if he were a stake [peg], not resting on one leg at a time and on the other at a time.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ يُصَلِّي صَافًّا بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ فِي مَا نَعْلَمُ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdi → Shu’bah → Sa’d ibn Ibrahim
english.text: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Sa’d ibn Ibrahim, who said: “I saw Ibn Umar praying, aligning his feet, as far as we know.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عِكْرِمَةَ يُصَلِّي صَافًّا بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdi → Isma’il ibn Muslim → Malik ibn Dinar
english.text: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Isma’il ibn Muslim, from Malik ibn Dinar, who said: “I saw Ikrimah praying, aligning his feet.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ حُبَابٍ، عَنْ قُرَيْشِ بْنِ حَيَّانَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ الْحَسَنَ يُصَلِّي صَافًّا بَيْنَ قَدَمَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Zayd ibn Hubab → Quraysh ibn Hayyan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Zayd ibn Hubab narrated to us, from Quraysh ibn Hayyan, who said: “I saw al-Hasan praying, aligning his feet.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنْ مُخْتَارِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ الْقَاسِمَ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ يَصُفُّ رِجْلَيْهِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ، وَلَا يُرَاوِحُ بَيْنَهُمَا

english.narrator: Ma’n ibn Isa → Mukhtar ibn Sa’d
english.text: Ma’n ibn Isa narrated to us, from Mukhtar ibn Sa’d, who said: “I saw al-Qasim ibn Muhammad aligning his legs in prayer, and he did not alternate between them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، قَالَ: "مَنْ دَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ وَقَدْ سُبِقَ بِالصَّلَاةِ، قَالَ: يَبْدَأُ بِالْمَكْتُوبَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Husayn → Sa’id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, from Husayn, from Sa’id ibn Jubayr, who said: “Whoever enters the mosque and has missed the prayer (Jama’ah), he said: He begins with the obligatory prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: يَبْدَأُ بِالْمَكْتُوبَةِ

english.narrator: Abbad ibn al-Awwam → Ibn Abi Najih → A Man → Ibn Umar
english.text: Abbad ibn al-Awwam narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Najih, from a man, from Ibn Umar, who said: “He begins with the obligatory prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، وَوَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: ابْدَأْ بِالَّذِي جِئْتَ لَهُ

english.narrator: Hushaym & Waki’ → Ibn Awn → Ash-Sha’bi
english.text: Hushaym and Waki’ narrated to us, from Ibn Awn, from ash-Sha’bi, who said: “Start with what you came for.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: ابْدَأْ بِالْمَكْتُوبَةِ

english.narrator: Hafs ibn Ghiyath → Ibn Awn → Ash-Sha’bi
english.text: Hafs ibn Ghiyath narrated to us, from Ibn Awn, from ash-Sha’bi, who said: “Start with the obligatory prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ الزُّبَيْرِ بْنِ عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: ابْدَأْ بِالْمَكْتُوبَةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Az-Zubayr ibn Adi → Ibrahim
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from az-Zubayr ibn Adi, from Ibrahim, who said: “Start with the obligatory prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَفْلَحَ، قَالَ: جِئْتُ أَنَا، وَالْقَاسِمُ الْمَسْجِدَ، وَقَدْ صَلَّوْا، فَصَلَّى لِنَفْسِهِ يَعْنِي بَدَأَ بِالْمَكْتُوبَةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Aflah
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Aflah, who said: “I and al-Qasim came to the mosque, and they had already prayed, so he prayed for himself,” meaning he started with the obligatory prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يَبْدَءُونَ بِالْمَكْتُوبَةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Shu’bah → Al-Hakam
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from al-Hakam, who said: “They used to start with the obligatory prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ الْحَكَمُ: كَانُوا يَبْدَءُونَ بِالْفَرِيضَةِ، وَقَالَ ابْنُ إِسْحَاقَ: كَانُوا يَبْدَءُونَ بِالْفَرِيضَةِ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu’bah → Al-Hakam
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu’bah, who said: Al-Hakam said: “They used to start with the obligatory prayer (Faridah).” And Ibn Ishaq said: “They used to start with the obligatory prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ ذُرَيْحٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: مَثَلُ الَّذِي يَدْخُلُ الْمَسْجِدَ وَقَدْ صَلَّى فِيهِ فَيَتَطَوَّعُ، مَثَلُ الَّذِي يَعْتَمِرُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَحُجَّ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Mis’ar → Al-Abbas ibn Dhurayh → Ibn Abbas
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Mis’ar, from al-Abbas ibn Dhurayh, from Ibn Abbas, who said: “The likeness of the one who enters the mosque when they have already prayed in it, and he performs voluntary prayer, is like the one who performs Umrah before he performs Hajj.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، قَالَ: ابْدَأْ بِالَّذِي جِئْتَ لَهُ

english.narrator: Abdul A’la → Ma’mar → Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad → Abdul Rahman ibn Abi Layla
english.text: Abdul A’la narrated to us, from Ma’mar, from Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad, from Abdul Rahman ibn Abi Layla, who said: “Start with what you came for.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ ذُرَيْحٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي رَجُلٍ دَخَلَ مَسْجِدًا، وَقَدْ صَلَّى أَهْلُهُ أَيَتَطَوَّعُ؟ قَالَ: هُوَ كَرَجُلٍ يَتَطَوَّعُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَحُجَّ

english.narrator: Ibn Mubarak → Mis’ar → Al-Abbas ibn Dhurayh → Ibn Abbas
english.text: Ibn Mubarak narrated to us, from Mis’ar, from al-Abbas ibn Dhurayh, from Ibn Abbas regarding a man who entered a mosque and its people had already prayed, should he perform voluntary prayer? He said: “He is like a man who performs voluntary worship before he performs Hajj.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ ذِرٍّ، قَالَ: يَتَطَوَّعُ إِنْ شَاءَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Husayn → Dhirr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, from Husayn, from Dhirr, who said: “He may perform voluntary prayer if he wishes.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنِ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي الْهُذَيْلِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَتَطَوَّعُ إِذَا سُبِقَ بِالْمَكْتُوبَةِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Al-Awwam → Abdullah ibn Abi al-Hudhayl
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from al-Awwam, from Abdullah ibn Abi al-Hudhayl: “That he used to perform voluntary prayer if he missed the obligatory prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ الْحَسَنُ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَدْخُلُ الْمَسْجِدَ، وَقَدْ صَلَّى أَهْلُهُ، قَالَ: فَقَالَ: أَمَّا أَنَا فَكُنْتُ أُصَلِّي لِمَا كُنْتُ أُصَلِّي قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Hafs → Ibn Awn → Al-Hasan
english.text: Hafs narrated to us, from Ibn Awn, who said: Al-Hasan was asked about a man who enters the mosque when its people have already prayed. He said: He said: “As for me, I would pray what I usually pray before it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ إِذَا كَانَ فِي وَقْتِ صَلَاةٍ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu’bah → Hammad
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Hammad, who said: “There is no harm in it if it is within the prayer time.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَخْبَرَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نَا شُعْبَةُ، قَالَ: قَالَ حَمَّادٌ: يَتَطَوَّعُ إِنْ شَاءَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Shu’bah → Hammad
english.text: Waki’ informed us, saying: Shu’bah narrated to us, saying: Hammad said: “He may perform voluntary prayer if he wishes.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا يُونُسُ بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو عُثْمَانَ الْيَشْكُرِيُّ، قَالَ: مَرَّ بِنَا أَنَسُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ، وَقَدْ صَلَّيْنَا صَلَاةَ الْغَدَاةِ، وَمَعَهُ رَهْطٌ، فَأَمَرَ رَجُلًا مِنْهُمْ فَأَذَّنَ، ثُمَّ صَلَّوْا رَكْعَتَيْنِ، قَبْلَ الْفَجْرِ، قَالَ: ثُمَّ أَمَرُوهُ، فَأَقَامَ، ثُمَّ تَقَدَّمَ فَصَلَّى بِهِمْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Yunus ibn Ubayd → Abu Uthman al-Yashkuri
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Yunus ibn Ubayd informed us, saying: Abu Uthman al-Yashkuri narrated to me, saying: “Anas ibn Malik passed by us, and we had already prayed the Morning prayer (Ghadah). He was with a group, so he ordered a man among them to call Adhan, then they prayed two Rak’ahs before Fajr (i.e. Sunnah). He said: Then they ordered him, and he called Iqbal (Iqamah), then he stepped forward and led them in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ الْجَعْدِ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، بِمِثْلِهِ

english.narrator: Isma’il ibn Ulayyah → Al-Ja’d Abu Uthman → Anas
english.text: Isma’il ibn Ulayyah narrated to us, from al-Ja’d Abu Uthman, from Anas, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنْ أَبِي حُرَّةَ، قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ أَنَا وَعَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ مَسْجِدًا وَقَدْ صُلِّيَ فِيهِ، قَالَ: أَلَا تَجِيءُ حَتَّى نُصَلِّيَ فِي جَمَاعَةٍ؟ قُلْتُ: إِنَّ بَعْضَهُمْ قَدْ كَرِهَ ذَلِكَ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَبِي لَا يَرَى بِذَلِكَ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Mu’adh ibn Mu’adh → Abu Hurrah
english.text: Mu’adh ibn Mu’adh narrated to us, from Abu Hurrah, who said: I and Abdullah ibn Humayd entered a mosque in which prayer had already been performed. He said: “Will you not come so we can pray in congregation?” I said: “Some people have disliked that.” He said: “My father saw no harm in that.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ النَّاجِي، عَنْ أَبِي الْمُتَوَكِّلِ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: جَاءَ رَجُلٌ وَقَدْ صَلَّى النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَقَالَ: أَيُّكُمْ يَتَّجِرُ عَلَى هَذَا؟ قَالَ: فَقَامَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ فَصَلَّى مَعَهُ

english.narrator: Abdah ibn Sulayman → Ibn Abi Arubah → Sulayman an-Naji → Abu al-Mutawakkil → Abu Sa’id
english.text: Abdah ibn Sulayman narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Arubah, from Sulayman an-Naji, from Abu al-Mutawakkil, from Abu Sa’id, who said: A man came after the Prophet ﷺ had already prayed. He said: “Who among you will trade (gain reward/charity) with this man?” He said: So a man from the people stood up and prayed with him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَنْبَأَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ التَّيْمِيُّ، عَنْ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ، قَالَ: دَخَلَ رَجُلٌ الْمَسْجِدَ وَقَدْ صَلَّى النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَقَالَ: أَلَا رَجُلٌ يَتَصَدَّقُ عَلَى هَذَا فَيَقُومُ فَيُصَلِّي مَعَهُ؟

english.narrator: Hushaym → Sulayman at-Taymi → Abu Uthman
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Sulayman at-Taymi informed us, from Abu Uthman, who said: A man entered the mosque after the Prophet ﷺ had prayed. He said: “Is there no man who will give charity to this one by standing up and praying with him?”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ مَعَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، مَسْجِدَ مُحَارِبٍ وَقَدْ صَلَّوْا فَأَمَّنِي

english.narrator: Sharik → Abdullah ibn Yazid
english.text: Sharik narrated to us, from Abdullah ibn Yazid, who said: “I entered the mosque of Muharib with Ibrahim, and they had already prayed, so he led me in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ زِيَادٍ، مَوْلَى قُرَيْشٍ، قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ مَعَ الْحَسَنِ مَسْجِدَ الْبَصْرَةِ، فَوَجَدْنَاهُمْ قَدْ صَلَّوْا فَصَلَّى بِي

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Ziyad
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, from Ziyad (Mawla of Quraysh), who said: “I entered the Mosque of Basra with al-Hasan, and we found they had already prayed, so he led me in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا أَنْ تُصَلَّى الْجَمَاعَةُ بَعْدَ الْجَمَاعَةِ فِي مَسْجِدِ الْكِلَاءِ بِالْبَصْرَةِ

english.narrator: Hafs → Ash’ath → Al-Hasan
english.text: Hafs narrated to us, from Ash’ath, from al-Hasan: “That he saw no harm in prayer being held congregation after congregation in the Mosque of al-Kila’ in Basra.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا مَنْصُورٌ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِنَّمَا كَانُوا يَكْرَهُونَ أَنْ يَجْمَعُوا مَخَافَةَ السُّلْطَانِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mansur → Al-Hasan
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Mansur informed us, from al-Hasan, who said: “They only used to dislike gathering [for a second congregation] out of fear of the ruler (Sultan).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مُسَافِرٍ الْجَصَّاصِ، عَنْ فُضَيْلِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، أَنَّ عَدِيَّ بْنَ ثَابِتٍ، وَأَصْحَابًا لَهُ رَجَعُوا مِنْ جِنَازَةٍ فَدَخَلُوا مَسْجِدًا، وَقَدْ صُلِّيَ فِيهِ فَجَمَعُوا فَكَرِهَ ذَلِكَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Musafir al-Jassas → Fudayl ibn ‘Amr → ‘Adi ibn Thabit
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Musafir al-Jassas, from Fudayl ibn ‘Amr, that ‘Adi ibn Thabit and companions of his returned from a funeral and entered a mosque where the prayer had already been performed, so they prayed in congregation (Jama‘ah). Ibrahim [al-Nakha‘i] disliked that.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ رَبِّهِ بْنِ أَبِي رَاشِدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى، قَالَ: جَاءَنَا أَنَسُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ، وَقَدْ صَلَّيْنَا الْغَدَاةَ، فَأَقَامَ الصَّلَاةَ، ثُمَّ صَلَّى بِهِمْ فَقَامَ وَسَطَهُمْ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → ‘Abd Rabbih ibn Abi Rashid → Yahya → Anas ibn Malik
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from ‘Abd Rabbih ibn Abi Rashid, who said: Yahya narrated to us, saying: Anas ibn Malik came to us after we had prayed the Morning Prayer (Al-Ghadah/Fajr). He established the prayer (gave Iqamah), then led them in prayer and stood in their middle.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، أَنَّهُ صَلَّى هُوَ وَسَالِمُ بْنُ عَطِيَّةَ، فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ فِي جَمَاعَةٍ، بَعْدَمَا صَلَّى أَهْلُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Mu‘awiyah → ‘Amr ibn Muhammad → ‘Ata’
english.text: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from ‘Amr ibn Muhammad, from ‘Ata’, that he and Salim ibn ‘Atiyyah prayed in the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) in congregation after its people had already prayed.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: يُصَلُّونَ جَمِيعًا فِي صَفٍّ وَاحِدٍ إِمَامُهُمْ وَسَطُهُمْ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Sa‘id → Qatadah
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Sa‘id, from Qatadah, that he said: "They pray all together in one row, and their Imam is in their middle."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ الْأَزْرَقُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ أَبِي سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ كُهَيْلٍ، أَنَّ ابْنَ مَسْعُودٍ دَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ وَقَدْ صَلَّوْا فَجَمَعَ بِعَلْقَمَةَ، وَمَسْرُوقٍ، وَالْأَسْوَدِ

english.narrator: Ishaq al-Azraq → ‘Abd al-Malik ibn Abi Sulayman → Salamah ibn Kuhayl → Ibn Mas‘ud
english.text: Ishaq al-Azraq narrated to us from ‘Abd al-Malik ibn Abi Sulayman, from Salamah ibn Kuhayl, that Ibn Mas‘ud entered the mosque after they had prayed, so he prayed in congregation with ‘Alqamah, Masruq, and Al-Aswad.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَنْ قَالَ: يُصَلُّونَ فُرَادَى، وَلَا يَجْمَعُونَ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: يُصَلُّونَ فُرَادَى

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: [Chapter: Who said: They pray individually and do not gather] Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, that he used to say: "They pray individually."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، قَالَ: يُصَلُّونَ فُرَادَى

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyub → Abu Qilabah
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Ayyub, from Abu Qilabah, who said: "They pray individually."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: يُصَلُّونَ فُرَادَى

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, who said: "They pray individually."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبِي هِلَالٍ، عَنْ كَثِيرٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَصْحَابُ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، إِذَا دَخَلُوا الْمَسْجِدَ وَقَدْ صُلِّيَ فِيهِ صَلَّوْا فُرَادَى

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Abu Hilal → Kathir → Al-Hasan
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Abu Hilal, from Kathir, from Al-Hasan, who said: "When the Companions of Muhammad ﷺ entered the mosque and the prayer had already been performed in it, they would pray individually."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَفْلَحَ، قَالَ: دَخَلْنَا مَعَ الْقَاسِمِ الْمَسْجِدَ وَقَدْ صُلِّيَ فِيهِ، قَالَ: فَصَلَّى الْقَاسِمُ وَحْدَهُ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Aflah → Al-Qasim
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Aflah, who said: We entered the mosque with Al-Qasim [ibn Muhammad] after the prayer had been performed in it. He said: "So Al-Qasim prayed alone."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الرَّجُلُ تَفُوتُهُ بَعْضُ الصَّلَاةِ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا مَنْصُورٌ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: "مَنْ أَدْرَكَ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، قَالَ: يَقْرَأُ فِيمَا أَدْرَكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Mansur → Al-Hasan → ‘Ali
english.text: [Chapter: The man who misses part of the prayer with the Imam] Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Mansur informed us, from Al-Hasan, from ‘Ali, that he used to say: "Whoever catches two Rak‘ahs with the Imam," he said, "he should recite in what he caught."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ، وَأَبَا الدَّرْدَاءِ، كَانَا يَقُولَانِ: مَا أَدْرَكْتَ مِنْ صَلَاةِ الْإِمَامِ فَاجْعَلْهُ أَوَّلَ صَلَاتِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Isma‘il ibn ‘Ayyash → Sa‘id ibn Abi ‘Abd al-Rahman → ‘Umar ibn al-Khattab + Abu al-Darda’
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Isma‘il ibn ‘Ayyash narrated to us from Sa‘id ibn Abi ‘Abd al-Rahman, that ‘Umar ibn al-Khattab and Abu al-Darda’ used to say: "Whatever you catch of the Imam's prayer, make it the beginning of your prayer."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُهَاجِرٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، يَقُولُ: اجْعَلْهُ أَوَّلَ صَلَاتِكَ

english.narrator: Isma‘il ibn ‘Ayyash → ‘Amr ibn Muhajir → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Aziz
english.text: Isma‘il ibn ‘Ayyash narrated to us from ‘Amr ibn Muhajir, who said: I heard ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Aziz say: "Make it the beginning of your prayer."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، وَالْحَسَنِ، قَالَا: مَا أَدْرَكْتَ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ فَهُوَ أَوَّلُ صَلَاتِكَ

english.narrator: ‘Abdah → Sa‘id → Qatadah → Sa‘id + Al-Hasan
english.text: ‘Abdah narrated to us from Sa‘id, from Qatadah, from Sa‘id and Al-Hasan, that they said: "Whatever you catch with the Imam is the beginning of your prayer."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: ‘Abdah → Ibn Abi ‘Arubah → Qatadah → ‘Ali
english.text: ‘Abdah narrated to us from Ibn Abi ‘Arubah, from Qatadah, from ‘Ali, the like of it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، قَالَ: كَانَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ، يَقُولُ: يَقْرَأُ فِيمَا أَدْرَكَ، لِأَنَّهُ كَانَ يُسِرُّ الْقِرَاءَةَ خَلْفَ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar → Al-A‘mash → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar narrated to us from Al-A‘mash, who said: Sa‘id ibn Jubayr used to say: "He recites in what he catches, because he used to recite silently behind the Imam."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، فِي الرَّجُلِ تَفُوتُهُ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ الرَّكْعَةُ، أَوِ الرَّكْعَتَانِ، قَالَ: يَقْرَأُ فِي سَكْتَةِ الْإِمَامِ وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ: مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar → Ash‘ath → Abu Ishaq → Al-Harith → ‘Ali
english.text: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar narrated to us from Ash‘ath, from Abu Ishaq, from Al-Harith, from ‘Ali, regarding a man who misses a Rak‘ah or two with the Imam, he said: "He recites during the Imam's silence." Al-Hasan said the same.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَنْ قَالَ: مَا أَدْرَكْتَ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ فَاجْعَلْهُ آخِرَ صَلَاتِكَ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، قَالَ: مَا أَدْرَكْتَ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ فَهُوَ آخِرُ صَلَاتِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Abdullah ibn Idris → Husayn → Ibrahim → ‘Abdullah
english.text: [Chapter: Who said: Whatever you catch with the Imam, make it the end of your prayer] Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abdullah ibn Idris narrated to us from Husayn, from Ibrahim, from ‘Abdullah [ibn Mas‘ud], who said: "Whatever you catch with the Imam is the end of your prayer."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ: اجْعَلْ آخِرَ صَلَاتِكَ مَا أَدْرَكْتَ مِنْ صَلَاتِكَ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Hammad ibn Salamah → Qatadah → Ibn Sirin → Ibn Mas‘ud
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Hammad ibn Salamah, from Qatadah, from Ibn Sirin, from Ibn Mas‘ud, who said: "Make what you caught of your prayer the end of your prayer."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَجْعَلُ مَا أَدْرَكَ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ آخِرَ صَلَاتِهِ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyub → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Ayyub, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he used to make what he caught with the Imam the end of his prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ إِذَا أَدْرَكَ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ لَمْ يَقْرَأْ فَإِذَا قَامَ يَقْضِي قَرَأَ

english.narrator: Hafs → ‘Ubaydullah → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Hafs narrated to us from ‘Ubaydullah, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that if he caught [the prayer] with the Imam, he would not recite [to himself as if it were the start], but when he stood up to make up [what he missed], he would recite.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: اقْرَأْ فِيمَا تَقْضِي

english.narrator: Abu Mu‘awiyah → ‘Ubaydullah → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from ‘Ubaydullah, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he said: "Recite in what you are making up."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا حُصَيْنٌ، وَمُغِيرَةُ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: يَقْرَأُ فِيمَا يَقْضِي

english.narrator: Hushaym → Husayn + Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Husayn and Mughirah informed us, from Ibrahim, who said: "He recites in what he makes up."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ مُجَاهِدًا عَنْ رَجُلٍ فَاتَتْهُ رَكْعَتَانِ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ فَقَرَأَ فِيهِمَا، قَالَ: اجْعَلْ آخِرَ صَلَاتِكَ أَوَّلَ صَلَاتِكَ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Ibn ‘Awn → Mujahid
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Ibn ‘Awn, who said: I asked Mujahid about a man who missed two Rak‘ahs with the Imam and recited in them [i.e., in the ones he caught, treating them as his first]. He said: "Make the end of your prayer [what you do with the Imam] the beginning of your prayer [i.e., treat what you caught as the start]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، فِي رَجُلٍ تَفُوتُهُ بَعْضُ الصَّلَاةِ، فَيَقُومُ يَقْضِي، قَالَ: يَجْعَلُ مَا بَقِيَ أَوَّلَ صَلَاتِهِ، وَإِنْ عَلِمْتَ مَا الَّذِي قَرَأَهُ الْإِمَامُ، فَاقْرَأْهُ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Wahhab → Ayyub → Abu Qilabah
english.text: ‘Abd al-Wahhab narrated to us from Ayyub, from Abu Qilabah, regarding a man who misses part of the prayer and stands to make it up. He said: "He makes what remains [what he is making up] the beginning of his prayer. And if you know what the Imam recited, then recite it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عَمْرَو بْنَ دِينَارٍ يَقُولُ: اقْضِ مَا فَاتَكَ كَمَا فَاتَكَ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar → ‘Amr ibn Dinar
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: I heard ‘Amr ibn Dinar say: "Make up what you missed exactly as you missed it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، وَابْنِ سِيرِينَ أَنَّهُمَا قَالَا فِيمَنْ سَبَقَهُ الْإِمَامُ: إِذَا قَضَيْتَ بَعْدَهُ، فَاقْضِ قِرَاءَتَكَ

english.narrator: Ya‘qub ibn Ibrahim → Ash‘ath → Al-Sha‘bi + Ibn Sirin
english.text: Ya‘qub ibn Ibrahim narrated to us from Ash‘ath, from Al-Sha‘bi and Ibn Sirin, that they said regarding one whom the Imam preceded: "If you make up [the prayer] after him, then make up your recitation."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، قَالَ: فَاتَتْ عُبَيْدَ بْنَ عُمَيْرٍ رَكْعَةٌ مِنَ الْمَغْرِبِ، فَسَمِعْتُهُ يَقْرَأُ وَاللَّيْلِ إِذَا يَغْشَى

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → ‘Amr → ‘Ubayd ibn ‘Umayr
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from ‘Amr, who said: ‘Ubayd ibn ‘Umayr missed a Rak‘ah of Maghrib, so I heard him recite 'By the night when it covers' (Surah Al-Layl) [when making it up].

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، قَالَ: كَانَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ يَقْرَأُ فِيمَا يَقْضِي

english.narrator: Abu Khalid → Al-A‘mash → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Khalid narrated to us from Al-A‘mash, who said: Ibrahim used to recite in what he was making up.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الرَّجُلُ يُصَلِّي فَيَضَعُ إِحْدَى رِجْلَيْهِ عَلَى الْأُخْرَى حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: يُكْرَهُ أَنْ يَرْفَعَ الرَّجُلُ إِحْدَى رِجْلَيْهِ عَلَى الْأُخْرَى فِي الصَّلَاةِ، أَوْ يَسْتَنِدُ إِلَى جِدَارٍ، إِلَّا مِنْ عِلَّةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: [Chapter: The man who prays putting one of his legs on the other] Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "It is disliked for a man to raise one of his legs onto the other during prayer, or to lean against a wall, unless it is due to an ailment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ هَانِئٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَمْرَو بْنَ مَيْمُونٍ قَائِمًا يُصَلِّي وَاضِعًا إِحْدَى رِجْلَيْهِ عَلَى الْأُخْرَى

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Yahya ibn Hani’ → ‘Amr ibn Maymun
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Yahya ibn Hani’, who said: "I saw ‘Amr ibn Maymun standing praying while placing one of his legs on the other."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا فِي الْإِمَامِ يُصَلِّي جَالِسًا حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَنَسًا، قَالَ: سَقَطَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنْ فَرَسٍ، فَجُحِشَ شِقُّهُ الْأَيْمَنُ، فَدَخَلْنَا عَلَيْهِ نَعُودُهُ، فَحَضَرَتِ الصَّلَاةُ، فَصَلَّى بِنَا قَاعِدًا، فَصَلَّيْنَا وَرَاءَهُ قُعُودًا، فَلَمَّا قَضَى الصَّلَاةَ قَالَ: "إِنَّمَا جُعِلَ الْإِمَامُ لِيُؤْتَمَّ بِهِ، فَإِذَا كَبَّرَ فَكَبِّرُوا، وَإِذَا رَكَعَ فَارْكَعُوا، وَإِذَا قَالَ: سَمِعَ اللَّهُ لِمَنْ حَمِدَهُ، فَقُولُوا: رَبَّنَا وَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ، وَإِذَا صَلَّى قَاعِدًا فَصَلُّوا قُعُودًا أَجْمَعُونَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sufyan ibn ‘Uyaynah → Al-Zuhri → Anas
english.text: [Chapter: Regarding the Imam praying while sitting] Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sufyan ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us from Al-Zuhri, who said: I heard Anas say: The Prophet ﷺ fell from a horse and his right side was scraped. We went in to visit him, and the time for prayer came, so he led us in prayer while sitting, and we prayed behind him sitting. When he finished the prayer, he said: "The Imam is appointed only to be followed. So when he says Takbir, say Takbir; when he bows, bow; when he says 'Allah hears whoever praises Him', say 'Our Lord, and to You is praise'; and if he prays sitting, then pray sitting all together."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتِ: اشْتَكَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَدَخَلَ نَاسٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ يَعُودُونَهُ، فَصَلَّى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ جَالِسًا، فَصَلُّوا بِصَلَاتِهِ قِيَامًا، وَأَشَارَ إِلَيْهِمْ أَنِ اجْلِسُوا فَجَلَسُوا، فَلَمَّا انْصَرَفَ قَالَ: إِنَّمَا جُعِلَ الْإِمَامُ لِيُؤْتَمَّ بِهِ، فَإِذَا رَكَعَ فَارْكَعُوا، وَإِذَا رَفَعَ فَارْفَعُوا، وَإِذَا صَلَّى جَالِسًا فَصَلُّوا جُلُوسًا

english.narrator: ‘Abdah → Hisham → His Father → ‘Aishah
english.text: ‘Abdah narrated to us from Hisham, from his father, from ‘Aishah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was in pain, so some people from his Companions came to visit him. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed sitting, and they prayed following his prayer standing. He gestured to them to sit, so they sat. When he finished, he said: "The Imam is appointed only to be followed. So when he bows, bow; when he rises, rise; and if he prays sitting, then pray sitting."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ أَبِي سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: صُرِعَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مِنْ فَرَسٍ لَهُ، فَوَقَعَ عَلَى جِذْعِ نَخْلَةٍ، فَانْفَكَّتْ قَدَمُهُ، فَدَخَلْنَا عَلَيْهِ نَعُودُهُ وَهُوَ يُصَلِّي فِي مَشْرُبَةٍ لِعَائِشَةَ، فَصَلَّيْنَا بِصَلَاتِهِ، وَنَحْنُ قِيَامٌ، ثُمَّ دَخَلْنَا عَلَيْهِ مَرَّةً أُخْرَى وَهُوَ يُصَلِّي جَالِسًا، فَصَلَّيْنَا بِصَلَاتِهِ وَنَحْنُ قِيَامٌ، فَأَوْمَأَ إِلَيْنَا أَنِ اجْلِسُوا، فَلَمَّا صَلَّى قَالَ: إِنَّمَا جُعِلَ الْإِمَامُ لِيُؤْتَمَّ بِهِ، فَإِذَا صَلَّى قَائِمًا فَصَلُّوا قِيَامًا، وَإِذَا صَلَّى جَالِسًا فَصَلُّوا جُلُوسًا، وَلَا تَقُومُوا وَهُوَ جَالِسٌ كَمَا يَفْعَلُ أَهْلُ فَارِسٍ بِعُظَمَائِهِمْ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Al-A‘mash → Abu Sufyan → Jabir
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Al-A‘mash narrated to us from Abu Sufyan, from Jabir, who said: The Prophet ﷺ was thrown from a horse of his and fell onto a palm trunk, dislocating his foot. We entered to visit him while he was praying in an upper room of ‘Aishah's. We prayed following his prayer while we were standing. Then we entered upon him another time while he was praying sitting, so we prayed following his prayer while we were standing. He gestured to us to sit. When he finished praying, he said: "The Imam is appointed only to be followed. If he prays standing, pray standing; and if he prays sitting, pray sitting. Do not stand while he is sitting as the people of Persia do with their great men."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَجْلَانَ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: "إِنَّمَا جُعِلَ الْإِمَامُ لِيُؤْتَمَّ بِهِ، فَإِذَا كَبَّرَ فَكَبِّرُوا، وَإِذَا قَرَأَ فَأَنْصِتُوا، وَإِذَا قَالَ: {غَيْرِ الْمَغْضُوبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا الضَّالِّينَ} [الفاتحة: 7] فَقُولُوا: آمِينَ، وَإِذَا رَكَعَ فَارْكَعُوا، وَإِذَا قَالَ: سَمِعَ اللَّهُ لِمَنْ حَمِدَهُ، فَقُولُوا: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّنَا لَكَ الْحَمْدُ، وَإِذَا سَجَدَ فَاسْجُدُوا، وَإِذَا صَلَّى جَالِسًا فَصَلُّوا جُلُوسًا

english.narrator: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar → Muhammad ibn ‘Ajlan → Zayd ibn Aslam → Abu Salih → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar narrated to us from Muhammad ibn ‘Ajlan, from Zayd ibn Aslam, from Abu Salih, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The Imam is appointed only to be followed. So when he says Takbir, say Takbir; when he recites, listen silently; when he says '{not of those who have evoked [Your] anger or of those who are astray}' [Al-Fatihah: 7], say 'Amin'; when he bows, bow; when he says 'Allah hears whoever praises Him', say 'O Allah, our Lord, to You is praise'; when he prostrates, prostrate; and if he prays sitting, pray sitting."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو الزُّبَيْرِ، أَنَّ جَابِرًا، اشْتَكَى عِنْدَهُمْ بِمَكَّةَ، فَلَمَّا أَنْ تَمَاثَلَ خَرَجَ، وَإِنَّهُمْ خَرَجُوا مَعَهُ يَتْبَعُونَهُ حَتَّى إِذَا بَلَغُوا بَعْضَ الطَّرِيقِ، حَضَرَتْ صَلَاةٌ مِنَ الصَّلَوَاتِ فَصَلَّى بِهِمْ جَالِسًا، وَصَلَّوْا مَعَهُ جُلُوسًا

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Wahhab al-Thaqafi → Yahya ibn Sa‘id → Abu al-Zubayr → Jabir
english.text: ‘Abd al-Wahhab al-Thaqafi narrated to us from Yahya ibn Sa‘id, who said: Abu al-Zubayr informed me that Jabir fell ill while with them in Makkah. When he recovered, he went out, and they went out with him following him. Until they reached part of the way, the time for one of the prayers arrived, so he led them in prayer sitting, and they prayed with him sitting.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ قَيْسٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: الْإِمَامُ أَمِيرٌ، فَإِنْ صَلَّى قَائِمًا فَصَلُّوا قِيَامًا، وَإِنْ صَلَّى قَاعِدًا فَصَلُّوا قُعُودًا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Isma‘il → Qays → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Isma‘il, from Qays, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: "The Imam is a commander (Amir). If he prays standing, pray standing; and if he prays sitting, pray sitting."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ فَهْدٍ، قَالَ: اشْتَكَى إِمَامُنَا فَصَلَّى قَاعِدًا فَصَلَّيْنَا بِصَلَاتِهِ، فَقَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ: الْإِمَامُ آمِينَ، فَإِنْ صَلَّى قَائِمًا فَصَلُّوا قِيَامًا، وَإِنْ صَلَّى قَاعِدًا فَصَلُّوا قُعُودًا

english.narrator: Abu Usamah → Isma‘il → Qays ibn Fahd → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Isma‘il, from Qays ibn Fahd, who said: Our Imam fell ill and prayed sitting, so we prayed following his prayer. Abu Hurayrah said: "The Imam is a trustee (Amin). If he prays standing, pray standing; and if he prays sitting, pray sitting."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ هُبَيْرَةَ، أَنَّ أُسَيْدَ بْنَ حُضَيْرٍ كَانَ يَؤُمُّ بَنِي عَبْدِ الْأَشْهَلِ، وَأَنَّهُ اشْتَكَى فَخَرَجَ إِلَيْهِمْ بَعْدَ شَكْوَاهُ، فَقَالُوا لَهُ: تَقَدَّمْ، قَالَ: لَا أَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ أُصَلِّيَ قَالُوا: لَا يَؤُمُّنَا أَحَدٌ غَيْرُكَ مَا دُمْتَ، فَقَالَ: اجْلِسُوا فَصَلَّى بِهِمْ جُلُوسًا

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Yahya ibn Sa‘id → ‘Abdullah ibn Hubayrah
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us from Yahya ibn Sa‘id, from ‘Abdullah ibn Hubayrah, that Usayd ibn Hudayr used to lead Banu ‘Abd al-Ashhal in prayer. He fell ill, and came out to them after his illness. They said to him: "Come forward [to lead]." He said: "I cannot pray [standing]." They said: "No one shall lead us but you as long as you are here." So he said: "Sit down," and he led them in prayer sitting.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا خَالِدُ بْنُ مَخْلَدٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ بِلَالٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ الْقَاسِمَ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ، يَقُولُ: قَالَ مُعَاوِيَةُ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: إِذَا صَلَّى الْأَمِيرُ جَالِسًا فَصَلُّوا جُلُوسًا قَالَ: فَعَجِبَ النَّاسُ مِنْ صِدْقِ مُعَاوِيَةَ

english.narrator: Khalid ibn Makhlad → Sulayman ibn Bilal → Ja‘far ibn Muhammad → Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad → Mu‘awiyah
english.text: Khalid ibn Makhlad narrated to us from Sulayman ibn Bilal, from Ja‘far ibn Muhammad, who said: I heard Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad saying: Mu‘awiyah said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "If the commander (Amir) prays sitting, then pray sitting." He said: "The people were amazed at Mu‘awiyah's truthfulness."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ أَبِي حَازِمٍ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ قَهَدٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ لَنَا إِمَامٌ فَمَرِضَ فَصَلَّيْنَا بِصَلَاتِهِ قُعُودًا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Isma‘il → Qays ibn Abi Hazim → Qays ibn Qahad
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Isma‘il narrated to us from Qays ibn Abi Hazim, from Qays ibn Qahad, who said: "We had an Imam who fell ill, so we prayed following his prayer sitting."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَنْ قَالَ: ائْتَمَّ بِالْإِمَامِ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْأَحْوَصِ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: إِنَّمَا جُعِلَ الْإِمَامُ لِيُؤْتَمَّ بِهِ، فَإِذَا كَبَّرَ فَكَبِّرُوا، وَإِذَا رَكَعَ فَارْكَعُوا، وَإِذَا سَجَدَ فَاسْجُدُوا، فَإِنَّهُ أَوَّلُ مَنْ يَرْفَعُ، وَأَوَّلُ مَنْ يَضَعُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu al-Ahwas → Abu Ishaq → Abu al-Ahwas → ‘Abdullah
english.text: [Chapter: Who said: Follow the Imam] Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu al-Ahwas narrated to us from Abu Ishaq, from Abu al-Ahwas, who said: ‘Abdullah [ibn Mas‘ud] said: "The Imam is appointed only to be followed. So when he says Takbir, say Takbir; when he bows, bow; when he prostrates, prostrate. For he is the first to rise and the first to lower himself."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَلْحَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ سُلَيْمَانُ: مَنْ رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ قَبْلَ الْإِمَامِ، وَوَضَعَ رَأْسَهُ قَبْلَ الْإِمَامِ فَنَاصِيَتُهُ بِيَدِ الشَّيْطَانِ يَرْفَعُهَا وَيَضَعُهَا

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn Fudayl → Layth → Talhah → Sulayman
english.text: Muhammad ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Layth, from Talhah, who said: Sulayman said: "Whoever raises his head before the Imam and lowers his head before the Imam, his forelock is in the hand of Satan; he raises it and lowers it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ مَلِيحِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ السَّعْدِيِّ، قَالَ: قَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ: إِنَّ الَّذِي يَخْفِضُ وَيَرْفَعُ رَأْسَهُ قَبْلَ الْإِمَامِ فَإِنَّمَا نَاصِيَتُهُ بِيَدِ الشَّيْطَانِ

english.narrator: ‘Abdah → Muhammad ibn ‘Amr → Malih ibn ‘Abdullah al-Sa‘di → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: ‘Abdah narrated to us from Muhammad ibn ‘Amr, from Malih ibn ‘Abdullah al-Sa‘di, who said: Abu Hurayrah said: "Indeed, the one who lowers and raises his head before the Imam, his forelock is only in the hand of Satan."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: أَمَا يَخَافُ الَّذِي يَرْفَعُ رَأْسَهُ قَبْلَ الْإِمَامِ، أَنْ يُحَوِّلَ اللَّهُ رَأْسَهُ رَأْسَ حِمَارٍ

english.narrator: Hammad ibn Salamah → Muhammad ibn Ziyad → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Does the one who raises his head before the Imam not fear that Allah might turn his head into the head of a donkey?"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ زِيَادِ بْنِ فَيَّاضٍ، عَنْ تَمِيمِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: أَمَا يَخَافُ الَّذِي يَرْفَعُ رَأْسَهُ قَبْلَ الْإِمَامِ، أَنْ يُحَوِّلَ اللَّهُ رَأْسَهُ رَأْسَ كَلْبٍ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Ziyad ibn Fayyad → Tamim ibn Salamah → ‘Abdullah
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Ziyad ibn Fayyad, from Tamim ibn Salamah, who said: ‘Abdullah [ibn Mas‘ud] said: "Does the one who raises his head before the Imam not fear that Allah might turn his head into the head of a dog?"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا الْعَوَّامُ، عَنْ عُزْرَةَ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ أَنَّهُ حَدَّثَهُ، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ بْنِ عَازِبٍ، قَالَ: كُنَّا إِذَا صَلَّيْنَا خَلْفَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَرَفَعْنَا رُءُوسَنَا مِنَ الرُّكُوعِ، قُمْنَا صُفُوفًا حَتَّى يَسْجُدَ فَإِذَا سَجَدَ تَبِعْنَاهُ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Al-‘Awwam → ‘Uzrah ibn al-Harith → Al-Bara’ ibn ‘Azib
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Al-‘Awwam informed us from ‘Uzrah ibn al-Harith, that he narrated to him from Al-Bara’ ibn ‘Azib, who said: "When we prayed behind the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and raised our heads from bowing (Ruku‘), we would remain standing in rows until he prostrated. Then, when he prostrated, we followed him."
