english.text: Jarir narrated to us, from Layth, from Sa’id ibn Jubayr, who said: “Five [things] diminish [the perfection of] prayer: Stretching, turning around, smoothing the pebbles, whispering [of the self/Satan], and cracking the fingers.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَعَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ: أَنَّهُمَا كَرِهَا أَنْ يُفَرْقِعَ الرَّجُلُ أَصَابِعَهُ وَهُوَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Hasan ibn Salih → Mughirah → Ibrahim & Layth → Mujahid
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Hasan ibn Salih, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, and from Layth, from Mujahid: “That they both disliked that a man crack his fingers while he is in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا حُصَيْنٌ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَرَى فِي ثَوْبِهِ دَمًا وَهُوَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ، قَالَ: إِنْ كَانَ كَثِيرًا فَلْيُلْقِ الثَّوْبَ عَنْهُ، وَإِنْ كَانَ قَلِيلًا فَلْيَمْضِ فِي صَلَاتِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Husayn → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Husayn informed us, saying: I asked Ibrahim about a man who sees blood on his garment while he is in prayer. He said: “If it is much, let him cast off the garment from him; and if it is little, let him proceed in his prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ وَرْدَانَ، عَنْ بُرْدٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ إِذَا كَانَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ فَرَأَى فِي ثَوْبِهِ دَمًا، فَإِنِ اسْتَطَاعَ أَنْ يَضَعَهُ وَضَعَهُ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ أَنْ يَضَعَهُ خَرَجَ فَغَسَلَهُ، ثُمَّ جَاءَ فَبَنَى عَلَى مَا كَانَ صَلَّى

english.narrator: Hatim ibn Wardan → Burd → Nafi’ → Ibn Umar
english.text: Hatim ibn Wardan narrated to us, from Burd, from Nafi’, from Ibn Umar: “That when he was in prayer and saw blood on his garment, if he could remove it, he would remove it. If he could not remove it, he would leave, wash it, then come back and build upon what he had prayed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَنْصَرِفُ مِنَ الدَّمِ قَلِيلِهِ وَكَثِيرِهِ

english.narrator: Ibn Numayr → Abdullah → Nafi’ → Ibn Umar
english.text: Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Abdullah, from Nafi’, from Ibn Umar: “That he used to leave [prayer] for blood, [whether] little or much.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ وَرْدَانَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِذَا رَأَيْتُهُ وَقَدْ صَلَّيْتُ بَعْضَ صَلَاتِكَ، فَضَعِ الثَّوْبَ عَنْكَ، وَامْضِ فِي صَلَاتِكَ

english.narrator: Hatim ibn Wardan → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Hatim ibn Wardan narrated to us, from Yunus, from al-Hasan, who said: “If you see it [blood] and you have prayed some of your prayer, take the garment off you and proceed in your prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ حَمَّادًا: عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي فَيَرَى فِي ثَوْبِهِ الدَّمَ، قَالَ: يُلْقِي الثَّوْبَ عَنْهُ، قُلْتُ: فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ إِلَّا ثَوْبَيْنِ، قَالَ: يُلْقِي أَحَدَهُمَا، وَيَتَوَشَّحُ بِالْآخَرِ، وَسَأَلَتْ الْحَكَمَ، فَقَالَ: مِثْلُ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu’bah → Hammad & Al-Hakam
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu’bah, who said: I asked Hammad about a man praying who sees blood on his garment. He said: “He casts off the garment from him.” I said: “What if there are only two garments?” He said: “He casts off one of them and wraps himself with the other.” And I asked al-Hakam, and he said the like of that.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ أَفْلَحَ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُصَلِّي فَرَأَى فِي ثَوْبِهِ دَمًا فَوَضَعَهُ

english.narrator: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn → Aflah → Al-Qasim
english.text: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us, from Aflah, from al-Qasim: “That he was praying and saw blood on his garment, so he removed it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي مِجْلَزٍ: فِي الدَّمِ يَكُونُ فِي الثَّوْبِ، فَقَالَ: إِذَا كَبَّرْتَ وَدَخَلْتَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ وَلَمْ تَرَ شَيْئًا، ثُمَّ رَأَيْتَهُ بَعْدُ فَأَتِمَّ الصَّلَاةَ

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Imran → Abu Mijlaz
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, from Imran, from Abu Mijlaz, regarding blood on the garment. He said: “If you said Takbir and entered the prayer and did not see anything, then you saw it afterwards, complete the prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا رَأَيْتَ فِي ثَوْبِكَ دَمًا فَامْضِ فِي صَلَاتِكَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Isra’il → Jabir → Abu Ja’far
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Isra’il, from Jabir, from Abu Ja’far, who said: “If you see blood on your garment, proceed in your prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْبَخْتَرِيِّ، عَنِ الْهُجَيْمِ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ رَبَاحٍ، أَرَى الدَّمَ فِي ثَوْبِي وَأَنَا فِي الصَّلَاةِ، قَالَ: امْضِ فِي صَلَاتِكَ، فَإِذَا انْصَرَفْتَ فَاغْسِلْهُ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Isra’il → Hammad ibn Salamah → Abu al-Bakhtari → Al-Hujaym → Abdullah ibn Rabah
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Isra’il, from Hammad ibn Salamah, from Abu al-Bakhtari, from al-Hujaym, who said: I said to Abdullah ibn Rabah: “I see blood on my garment while I am in prayer.” He said: “Proceed in your prayer, and when you finish, wash it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْأَوْزَاعِيِّ، عَنْ خُصَيْفٍ الْجَزَرِيِّ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: رَخَّصَ الشَّيْخُ إِذَا أَرَادَ الْقِيَامَ لِلصَّلَاةِ أَنْ يُقَدِّمَ رِجْلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Isa ibn Yunus → Al-Awza’i → Khusayf al-Jazari → Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Isa ibn Yunus narrated to us, from al-Awza’i, from Khusayf al-Jazari, from Mujahid, who said: “The Shaykh granted a concession that if one wants to stand up for prayer, he may put one leg forward.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ السُّلَمِيِّ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مَعْبَدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَنْهَضُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ، فَيُقَدِّمُ إِحْدَى رِجْلَيْهِ: فَكَرِهَهُ، وَقَالَ: هَذِهِ خُطْوَةٌ مَلْعُونَةٌ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Muhammad ibn Ali as-Sulami → Ibrahim ibn Ma’bad → Ibn Abbas
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Ali as-Sulami, from Ibrahim ibn Ma’bad, from Ibn Abbas, regarding a man who rises in prayer and puts one of his legs forward. He disliked it and said: “This is a cursed step.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو بَكْرٍ، عَمَّنْ أَخْبَرَهُ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَهَى أَنْ يُخَمَّرَ الْفَمُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Usamah → Ibn Jurayj → Abu Bakr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Usamah narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: Abu Bakr informed me, from one who informed him: “That the Prophet ﷺ forbade covering the mouth in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَحَدَّثَنِي الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُغَطِّيَ الرَّجُلُ فَاهُ وَهُوَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Ath-Thaqafi → Muhammad
english.text: Ath-Thaqafi narrated to me, from Muhammad: “That he used to dislike that a man cover his mouth while he is in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يُغَطِّيَ الرَّجُلُ فَمَهُ وَهُوَ فِي صَلَاةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Dawud → Shu’bah → Mansur → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Dawud narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Mansur, from Ibrahim: “That he disliked that a man cover his mouth while he is in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ هِلَالِ بْنِ يَسَافَ، عَنْ جَعْدَةَ بْنِ هُبَيْرَةَ: أَنَّهُ رَأَى رَجُلًا يُصَلِّي وَعَلَيْهِ مِغْفَرٌ وَعِمَامَةٌ قَدْ غَطَّى بِهِمَا وَجْهَهُ فَأَخَذَ بِمِغْفَرِهِ وَعِمَامَتِهِ فَأَلْقَاهُمَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ

english.narrator: Ibn Fudayl → Husayn → Hilal ibn Yasaf → Ja’dah ibn Hubayrah
english.text: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Husayn, from Hilal ibn Yasaf, from Ja’dah ibn Hubayrah: “That he saw a man praying wearing a helmet [or coif] and a turban with which he had covered his face. So he took his helmet and turban and cast them behind him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ تَغْطِيَةِ الْفَمِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ وَالطَّوَافِ، فَكَرِهَهُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ، وَرَخَّصَ فِيهِ فِي الطَّوَافِ

english.narrator: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar → Hajjaj → Ata
english.text: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar narrated to us, from Hajjaj, from Ata, who said: “I asked him about covering the mouth in prayer and Tawaf. He disliked it in prayer, and granted a concession for it in Tawaf.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْمُجْبِرِ، أَنَّ سَالِمَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ: كَانَ إِذَا رَأَى الرَّجُلَ يُغَطِّي فَاهُ وَهُوَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ جَبَذَ الثَّوْبَ جَبْذًا شَدِيدًا حَتَّى يَنْزِعَهُ مِنْ فِيهِ

english.narrator: Ma’n ibn Isa → Malik ibn Anas → Abdul Rahman ibn al-Mujbir → Salim ibn Abdullah
english.text: Ma’n ibn Isa narrated to us, from Malik ibn Anas, from Abdul Rahman ibn al-Mujbir, that Salim ibn Abdullah: “If he saw a man covering his mouth while in prayer, he would pull the garment forcefully until he removed it from his mouth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يُغَطِّيَ الرَّجُلُ فَمَهُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Hajjaj → Ata
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, from Hajjaj, from Ata: “That he disliked that a man cover his mouth in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَمَّنْ سَمِعَ ابْنَ أَبِي لَيْلَى، يَقُولُ مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Hajjaj → One who heard Ibn Abi Layla
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, from Hajjaj, from one who heard Ibn Abi Layla, saying the like of it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَزْهَرُ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مُسْلِمِ بْنِ بُدَيْلٍ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ هَكَذَا وَوَضَعَ أَزْهَرُ ثَوْبَهُ عَلَى شَفَتِهِ

english.narrator: Azhar → Ibn Awn → Muslim ibn Budayl
english.text: Azhar narrated to us, from Ibn Awn, from Muslim ibn Budayl: “That he used to dislike praying like this,” and Azhar placed his garment over his lip.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا بُكَيْرٌ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَالشَّعْبِيِّ: أَنَّهُمَا كَرِهَا أَنْ يُغَطِّيَ الرَّجُلُ فَاهُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Bukayr → Amir → Ibrahim & Ash-Sha’bi
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Bukayr narrated to us, from Amir, from Ibrahim and ash-Sha’bi: “That they both disliked that a man cover his mouth in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْعُمَرِيُّ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يَتَلَثَّمَ الرَّجُلُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki’ → Al-Umari → Nafi’ → Ibn Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Al-Umari narrated to us, from Nafi’, from Ibn Umar: “That he disliked that a man veil [cover the lower face] himself in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامٌ الدَّسْتُوَائِيُّ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، وَعِكْرِمَةَ: أَنَّهُمَا كَرِهَا أَنْ يَتَلَثَّمَ الرَّجُلُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Hisham ad-Dastuwa’i → Qatadah → Sa’id ibn al-Musayyib & Ikrimah
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Hisham ad-Dastuwa’i narrated to us, from Qatadah, from Sa’id ibn al-Musayyib and Ikrimah: “That they both disliked that a man veil himself in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ الرَّجُلُ مُتَلَثِّمًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Layth → Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs narrated to us, from Layth, from Tawus: “That he disliked that a man pray while veiled.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يَتَلَثَّمَ الرَّجُلُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Shu’bah → Al-Hakam → Ibrahim
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from al-Hakam, from Ibrahim: “That he disliked that a man veil himself in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ مُتَلَثِّمًا

english.narrator: Hafs → Ash’ath → Al-Hasan
english.text: Hafs narrated to us, from Ash’ath, from al-Hasan: “That he disliked for a man to pray while veiled.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، قَالَ: "كَانَ يُكْرَهُ التَّلَثُّمُ فِي ثَلَاثٍ: فِي الْقِتَالِ، وَفِي الْجَنَائِزِ، وَفِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Ata ibn as-Sa’ib
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Ata ibn as-Sa’ib, who said: “Veiling was disliked in three [situations]: In fighting, in funerals, and in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ الِالْتِثَامَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ عَلَى الْأَنْفِ وَالْفَمِ

english.narrator: Abdul A’la → Khalid → A Man → Ali
english.text: Abdul A’la narrated to us, from Khalid, from a man, from Ali: “That he disliked veiling over the nose and mouth in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يُغَطِّي أَنْفَهُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ، فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عِكْرِمَةُ، أَنَّ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ كَرِهَ تَغْطِيَةَ الْأَنْفِ، قَالَ قَتَادَةُ: وَكَانَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ الْمُسَيِّبِ، وَالنَّخَعِيُّ، وَعَطَاءٌ يَكْرَهُونَهُ، وَكَانَ الْحَسَنُ: لَا يَرَى بِهِ بَأْسًا، قَالَ قَتَادَةُ: فَأَمَّا الْفَمُ فَلَا أَرَى بِهِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Abu Dawud at-Tayalisi → Hisham → Qatadah → Ikrimah → Ibn Abbas
english.text: Abu Dawud at-Tayalisi narrated to us, from Hisham, from Qatadah, regarding a man covering his nose in prayer. He said: Ikrimah told me that Ibn Abbas disliked covering the nose. Qatadah said: And Sa’id ibn al-Musayyib, an-Nakha’i, and Ata used to dislike it. But al-Hasan saw no harm in it. Qatadah said: As for the mouth, I see no harm in it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ أَبِي خَلْدَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يُغَطِّيَ أَنْفَهُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Abu Dawud → Abu Khaldah → Abu al-Aliyah
english.text: Abu Dawud narrated to us, from Abu Khaldah, from Abu al-Aliyah: “That he disliked covering his nose in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ حَمَّادًا: فَكَرِهَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Dawud → Shu’bah → Hammad
english.text: Abu Dawud narrated to us, from Shu’bah, who said: I asked Hammad: “[And] he disliked it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: كَانَ يَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُغَطِّيَ أَنْفَهُ وَفَمَهُ جَمِيعًا، وَلَا يَرَى بَأْسًا أَنْ يُغَطِّيَ فَمَهُ دُونَ أَنْفِهِ

english.narrator: Hafs → Shu’bah → Qatadah → Al-Hasan
english.text: Hafs narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Qatadah, from al-Hasan, who said: “He used to dislike covering his nose and mouth together, but saw no harm in covering his mouth without his nose.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي رَافِعٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ كَعْبٍ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ الْمَرْأَةُ وَهِيَ مُنْتَقِبَةٌ أَوْ تَطُوفَ وَهِيَ مُنْتَقِبَةٌ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Abdul Hamid ibn Abi Rafi’ → Sa’id ibn Ka’b → Jabir ibn Zayd
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Abdul Hamid ibn Abi Rafi’, from Sa’id ibn Ka’b, from Jabir ibn Zayd: “That he disliked that a woman pray while wearing a Niqab [face veil] or perform Tawaf while wearing a Niqab.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ الْمَرْأَةُ وَهِيَ مُنْتَقِبَةٌ

english.narrator: Hafs → Layth → Tawus
english.text: Hafs narrated to us, from Layth, from Tawus: “That he disliked that a woman pray while wearing a Niqab.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: كَانَ يَكْرَهُ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ الْمَرْأَةُ مُنْتَقِبَةً

english.narrator: Hafs → Ash’ath → Al-Hasan
english.text: Hafs narrated to us, from Ash’ath, from al-Hasan, who said: “He used to dislike that a woman pray wearing a Niqab.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ ضَمْرَةَ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، سَمِعَ أَبَا سَعِيدٍ، يَقُولُ: نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنْ صَلَاةٍ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ حَتَّى الْغُرُوبِ، وَبَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ حَتَّى الطُّلُوعِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sufyan ibn Uyaynah → Damrah ibn Sa’id → Abu Sa’id
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sufyan ibn Uyaynah narrated to us, from Damrah ibn Sa’id, who heard Abu Sa’id saying: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade prayer after Asr until sunset, and after Fajr until sunrise.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ نَضْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ مُعَاذٍ الْقُرَشِيِّ أَنَّهُ طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ مَعَ مُعَاذِ ابْنِ عَفْرَاءَ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ وَبَعْدَ الصُّبْحِ، فَلَمْ يُصَلِّ، فَسَأَلْتُهُ، فَقَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: "لَا صَلَاةَ بَعْدَ صَلَاتَيْنِ: بَعْدَ الْغَدَاةِ حَتَّى تَطْلُعَ الشَّمْسُ، وَبَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ حَتَّى تَغْرُبَ الشَّمْسُ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu’bah → Sa’d ibn Ibrahim → Nadr ibn Abdul Rahman → Mu’adh al-Qurashi
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Sa’d ibn Ibrahim, from Nadr ibn Abdul Rahman, from his grandfather Mu’adh al-Qurashi: That he performed Tawaf around the House with Mu’adh ibn Afra’ after Asr and after Subh [Fajr], but he did not pray. I asked him, and he said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “There is no prayer after two prayers: After the Morning prayer [Ghadah] until the sun rises, and after Asr until the sun sets.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، وَابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، عَنْ حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَفْصِ بْنِ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: "نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنْ صَلَاتَيْنِ: عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ بَعْدِ طُلُوعِ الْفَجْرِ حَتَّى تَطْلُعَ الشَّمْسُ، وَبَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ حَتَّى تَغْرُبَ الشَّمْسُ

english.narrator: Abu Usamah & Ibn Numayr → Abdullah ibn Umar → Habib → Abdul Rahman → Hafs ibn Asim → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Usamah and Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Abdullah ibn Umar, from Habib, from Abdul Rahman, from Hafs ibn Asim, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade two prayers: Prayer after the rising of dawn until the sun rises, and after Asr until the sun sets.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، وَابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَتْنِي عَمْرَةُ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: "نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنْ صَلَاتَيْنِ: عَنْ صَلَاةٍ بَعْدَ طُلُوعِ الْفَجْرِ حَتَّى تَطْلُعَ الشَّمْسُ وَتَرْتَفِعَ، فَإِنَّهَا تَطْلُعُ بَيْنَ قَرْنَيْ شَيْطَانٍ، وَعَنْ صَلَاةٍ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ حَتَّى تَغِيبَ الشَّمْسُ، فَإِنَّهَا تَغِيبُ بَيْنَ قَرْنَيْ شَيْطَانٍ

english.narrator: Abu Usamah & Ibn Numayr → Sa’id ibn Sa’id → Amrah → Aishah
english.text: Abu Usamah and Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Sa’id ibn Sa’id, who said: Amrah informed me, from Aishah, who said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade two prayers: Prayer after the rising of dawn until the sun rises and rises high, for it rises between the horns of a devil; and prayer after Asr until the sun disappears, for it disappears between the horns of a devil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ هِلَالِ بْنِ يَسَافَ، عَنْ وَهْبِ بْنِ الْأَجْدَعِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَا صَلَاةَ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونَ الشَّمْسُ بَيْضَاءَ نَقِيَّةً

english.narrator: Jarir → Mansur → Hilal ibn Yasaf → Wahb ibn al-Ajda’ → Ali
english.text: Jarir narrated to us, from Mansur, from Hilal ibn Yasaf, from Wahb ibn al-Ajda’, from Ali, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “There is no prayer after Asr unless the sun is white and clear [still high].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ سِمَاكٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ الْمُهَلَّبَ بْنَ أَبِي صُفْرَةَ يُحَدِّثُ، عَنْ سَمُرَةَ بْنِ جُنْدُبٍ: أَنَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: "لَا تُصَلُّوا، أَوْ قَالَ: نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ بَعْدَ صَلَاةِ الصُّبْحِ حَتَّى تَطْلُعَ الشَّمْسُ، فَإِنَّهَا تَطْلُعُ عَلَى قَرْنٍ، أَوْ بَيْنَ قَرْنَيْ شَيْطَانٍ

english.narrator: Abu Dawud → Shu’bah → Simak → Al-Muhallab ibn Abi Sufrah → Samurah ibn Jundub
english.text: Abu Dawud narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Simak, who said: I heard al-Muhallab ibn Abi Sufrah narrating from Samurah ibn Jundub: That the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “Do not pray,” or he said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade praying after the Morning prayer [Subh] until the sun rises, for it rises on a horn, or between the horns of a devil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَبَابَةُ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي التَّيَّاحِ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ حُمْرَانَ بْنَ أَبَانَ يُحَدِّثُ، عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ: أَنَّهُ نَظَرَ إِلَى أُنَاسٍ يُصَلُّونَ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، فَقَالَ: إِنَّكُمْ تُصَلُّونَ صَلَاةً قَدْ صَحِبْنَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَمَا رَأَيْنَاهُ يُصَلِّيهَا وَقَدْ نَهَى عَنْهَا

english.narrator: Shababah → Shu’bah → Abu at-Tayyah → Humran ibn Aban → Mu’awiyah
english.text: Shababah narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Abu at-Tayyah, who said: I heard Humran ibn Aban narrating from Mu’awiyah: That he looked at people praying after Asr and said: “You are praying a prayer; we accompanied the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and we never saw him praying it, and indeed he forbade it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ حُسَيْنٍ الْمُعَلِّمِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ: أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَهَى عَنْ صَلَاةٍ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ حَتَّى تَغْرُبَ الشَّمْسُ، وَعَنْ صَلَاةٍ بَعْدَ الصُّبْحِ حَتَّى تَطْلُعَ الشَّمْسُ

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Husayn al-Mu’allim → Amr ibn Shu’ayb → His Father → His Grandfather
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, from Husayn al-Mu’allim, from Amr ibn Shu’ayb, from his father, from his grandfather: “That the Prophet ﷺ forbade prayer after Asr until the sun sets, and prayer after Subh (Morning) until the sun rises.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُوسَى، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ: "أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَهَى عَنْ صَلَاتَيْنِ: عَنْ صَلَاةٍ بَعْدَ الصُّبْحِ حَتَّى تَطْلُعَ الشَّمْسُ، وَعَنْ صَلَاةٍ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ حَتَّى تَغْرُبَ الشَّمْسُ

english.narrator: Ubayd Allah ibn Musa → Musa ibn Ubaydah → Nafi’ → Ibn Umar
english.text: Ubayd Allah ibn Musa narrated to us, from Musa ibn Ubaydah, from Nafi’, from Ibn Umar: “That the Prophet ﷺ forbade two prayers: Prayer after Subh until the sun rises, and prayer after Asr until the sun sets.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هَمَّامٌ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا رِجَالٌ مَرْضِيُّونَ فِيهِمْ عُمَرُ أَرْضَاهُمْ عِنْدِي عُمَرُ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: لَا صَلَاةَ بَعْدَ الصُّبْحِ حَتَّى تَطْلُعَ الشَّمْسُ، وَلَا صَلَاةَ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ حَتَّى تَغْرُبَ الشَّمْسُ

english.narrator: Affan → Hammam → Qatadah → Abu al-Aliyah → Ibn Abbas → Umar
english.text: Affan narrated to us, saying: Hammam narrated to us, from Qatadah, from Abu al-Aliyah, from Ibn Abbas, who said: Men whom I am pleased with narrated to me—among them was Umar, and the most pleasing of them to me is Umar—that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “There is no prayer after Subh until the sun rises, and there is no prayer after Asr until the sun sets.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنِ الْمُهَاجِرِ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ قَالَ: لَا تَصِحُّ الصَّلَاةُ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ حَتَّى تَغِيبَ الشَّمْسُ، وَبَعْدَ الصُّبْحِ حَتَّى تَطْلُعَ الشَّمْسُ قَالَ: وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ يَضْرِبُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Ath-Thaqafi → Al-Muhajir → Abu al-Aliyah
english.text: Ath-Thaqafi narrated to us, from al-Muhajir, from Abu al-Aliyah, who said: “Prayer is not valid after Asr until the sun disappears, and after Subh until the sun rises.” He said: “And Ibn Umar used to hit [people] for that.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ شَدَّادٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنِ الْأَشْتَرِ قَالَ: كَانَ خَالِدُ بْنُ الْوَلِيدِ يَضْرِبُ النَّاسَ عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn Fudayl → Al-Hasan ibn Ubayd Allah → Muhammad ibn Shaddad → Abdul Rahman ibn Yazid → Al-Ashtar
english.text: Muhammad ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from al-Hasan ibn Ubayd Allah, from Muhammad ibn Shaddad, from Abdul Rahman ibn Yazid, from al-Ashtar, who said: “Khalid ibn al-Walid used to hit people for praying after Asr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، وَوَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ شَقِيقٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ: أَنَّ عُمَرَ كَرِهَ الصَّلَاةَ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، وَإِنِّي أَكْرَهُ مَا كَرِهَ عُمَرُ

english.narrator: Abu Mu’awiyah & Waki’ → Al-A’mash → Shaqiq → Abdullah
english.text: Abu Mu’awiyah and Waki’ narrated to us, from al-A’mash, from Shaqiq, from Abdullah: “That Umar disliked prayer after Asr, and indeed I dislike what Umar disliked.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ شَقِيقٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عُمَرَ أَبْصَرَ رَجُلًا يُصَلِّي بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، فَضَرَبَهُ حَتَّى سَقَطَ رِدَاؤُهُ

english.narrator: Ibn Fudayl → Husayn → Abdullah ibn Shaqiq
english.text: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Husayn, from Abdullah ibn Shaqiq, who said: “I saw Umar catch sight of a man praying after Asr, so he hit him until his cloak fell off.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ حُبَابٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي سَارَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ سَالِمًا عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، فَقَالَ: مَا أُحِبُّ أَنْ أَبْتَدِئَ بِصَلَاةٍ حَتَّى تَغْرُبَ الشَّمْسُ

english.narrator: Zayd ibn Hubab → Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abi Sarah → Salim
english.text: Zayd ibn Hubab narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abi Sarah, who said: I asked Salim about prayer after Asr. He said: “I do not like to start a prayer until the sun sets.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: كَانَ يَكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةَ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ حَتَّى تَغْرُبَ الشَّمْسُ

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Hisham → Ibn Sirin
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, from Hisham, from Ibn Sirin, who said: “He used to dislike prayer after Asr until the sun sets.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي جَمْرَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عُمَرَ يَضْرِبُ عَلَى الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Shu’bah → Abu Jamrah → Ibn Abbas
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Abu Jamrah, from Ibn Abbas, who said: “I saw Umar hitting [people] for the two Rak’ahs after Asr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عُبَيْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ رَافِعِ بْنِ خَدِيجٍ يُحَدِّثُ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: رَآنِي عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ يَوْمًا وَأَنَا أُصَلِّي بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، فَانْتَظَرَنِي حَتَّى صَلَّيْتُ، فَقَالَ: مَا هَذِهِ الصَّلَاةُ؟ فَقُلْتُ: سَبَقْتنِي بِشَيْءٍ مِنَ الصَّلَاةِ، فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: لَوْ عَلِمْتُ أَنَّكَ تُصَلِّي بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ لَفَعَلْتُ وَفَعَلْتُ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Shu’bah → Sa’d ibn Ibrahim → Ubayd Allah ibn Rafi’ ibn Khadij → His Father
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Sa’d ibn Ibrahim, who said: I heard Ubayd Allah ibn Rafi’ ibn Khadij narrating from his father, who said: Umar ibn al-Khattab saw me one day while I was praying after Asr. He waited for me until I finished praying, then he said: “What is this prayer?” I said: “I missed something from the prayer.” Umar said: “If I had known that you were praying [voluntarily] after Asr, I would have done such and such [punished you].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ثَابِتٌ، عَنْ عُمَارَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي تَمِيمَةَ الْهُجَيْمِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَمَعَ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، وَعُمَرَ، وَعُثْمَانَ فَلَا صَلَاةَ بَعْدَ الْغَدَاةِ حَتَّى تَطْلُعَ الشَّمْسُ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Thabit → Umarah → Abu Tamimah al-Hujaymi → Ibn Umar
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Thabit narrated to us, from Umarah, from Abu Tamimah al-Hujaymi, from Ibn Umar, who said: “I prayed with the Prophet ﷺ, and with Abu Bakr, Umar, and Uthman, and there is no prayer after the Morning prayer (Ghadah) until the sun rises.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ ضَمْرَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُصَلِّي عَلَى أَثَرِ كُلِّ صَلَاةٍ مَكْتُوبَةٍ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، إِلَّا الْفَجْرَ وَالْعَصْرَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Abu Ishaq → Asim ibn Damrah → Ali
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Abu Ishaq, from Asim ibn Damrah, from Ali, who said: “The Prophet ﷺ used to pray two Rak’ahs after every obligatory prayer, except for Fajr and Asr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنِ السَّائِبِ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ يَضْرِبُ الْمُنْكَدِرَ عَلَى السَّجْدَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ يَعْنِي الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Ibn Abi Dhi’b → Az-Zuhri → As-Sa’ib
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Ibn Abi Dhi’b narrated to us, from az-Zuhri, from as-Sa’ib, who said: “I saw Umar ibn al-Khattab hitting al-Munkadir for the two prostrations after Asr,” meaning the two Rak’ahs.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، عَنْ زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ، عَنْ سُوَيْدٍ، وَعَنْ أَبِي حُسَيْنٍ، عَنْ قَبِيصَةَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ عُمَرُ يَضْرِبُ عَلَى الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ

english.narrator: Husayn ibn Ali → Za’idah → Imran & Abu Husayn → Suwayd & Qabisah ibn Jabir
english.text: Husayn ibn Ali narrated to us, from Za’idah, from Imran, from Suwayd, and from Abu Husayn, from Qabisah ibn Jabir, who said: “Umar used to hit [people] for the two Rak’ahs after Asr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الْمُخْتَارِ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَنَسَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ، فَقَالَ: كَانَ عُمَرُ يَضْرِبُ الْأَيْدِي عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ

english.narrator: Ibn Fudayl → Al-Mukhtar → Anas ibn Malik
english.text: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from al-Mukhtar, who said: I asked Anas ibn Malik about prayer after Asr. He said: “Umar used to hit the hands for prayer after Asr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو هِلَالٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ بُرَيْدَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: تَمْرَتَانِ بِزُبْدٍ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ صَلَاةٍ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Abu Hilal → Ibn Buraydah → Abu Sa’id
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Abu Hilal narrated to us, from Ibn Buraydah, from Abu Sa’id, who said: “Two dates with butter are more beloved to me than a prayer after Asr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ طَلْقٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْبَيْلَمَانِيِّ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَنْبَسَةَ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، هَلْ مِنْ سَاعَةٍ أَقْرَبُ إِلَى اللَّهِ مِنْ سَاعَةٍ؟ فَقَالَ: نَعَمْ، جَوْفُ اللَّيْلِ فَصَلِّ مَا بَدَا لَكَ حَتَّى تُصَلِّيَ الصُّبْحَ، ثُمَّ انْهَهُ حَتَّى تَطْلُعَ الشَّمْسُ، وَمَا دَامَتْ كَأَنَّهَا جُحْفَةٌ حَتَّى تَنْتَشِرَ، ثُمَّ صَلِّ مَا بَدَا لَكَ حَتَّى يَقُومَ الْعَمُودُ عَلَى ظِلِّهِ، ثُمَّ انْهَهُ حَتَّى تَزُولَ الشَّمْسُ، فَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ تَسْخُنُ نِصْفَ النَّهَارِ، ثُمَّ صَلِّ مَا بَدَا لَكَ حَتَّى تُصَلِّيَ الْعَصْرَ، ثُمَّ انْهَهُ حَتَّى تَغْرُبَ الشَّمْسُ، فَإِنَّهَا تَطْلُعُ بَيْنَ قَرْنَيْ شَيْطَانٍ وَتَغْرُبُ بَيْنَ قَرْنَيْ شَيْطَانٍ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu’bah → Ya’la ibn Ata → Yazid ibn Talq → Abdul Rahman ibn al-Baylamani → Amr ibn Abasah
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Ya’la ibn Ata, from Yazid ibn Talq, from Abdul Rahman ibn al-Baylamani, from Amr ibn Abasah, who said: I said: “O Messenger of Allah, is there any hour closer to Allah than another hour?” He said: “Yes, the middle of the night. So pray as appears to you until you pray Subh (Morning), then stop until the sun rises and as long as it looks like a shield until it spreads [brightens]. Then pray as appears to you until the pole stands on its shadow [zenith], then stop until the sun passes its zenith, for Hell is heated at midday. Then pray as appears to you until you pray Asr, then stop until the sun sets, for it rises between the horns of a devil and sets between the horns of a devil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: مَا تَرَكَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ رَكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ فِي بَيْتِي قَطُّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki’ → Hisham ibn Urwah → His Father → Aishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki’ narrated to us, from Hisham ibn Urwah, from his father, from Aishah, who said: “The Prophet ﷺ never left two Rak’ahs after Asr in my house ever.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ مَعَ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ عَلَى مُعَاوِيَةَ فَأَجْلَسَهُ مُعَاوِيَةُ عَلَى السَّرِيرِ، ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ: مَا رَكْعَتَانِ يُصَلِّيهِمَا النَّاسُ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ لَمْ نَرَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ صَلَّاهُمَا، وَلَا أَمَرَ بِهِمَا، قَالَ: ذَلِكَ مَا يُفْتِي بِهِ النَّاسَ ابْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ، فَأَرْسَلَ إِلَى ابْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ فَسَأَلَهُ فَقَالَ: أَخْبَرَتْنِي ذَلِكَ عَائِشَةُ، فَأَرْسَلَ إِلَى عَائِشَةَ فَقَالَتْ: أَخْبَرَتْنِي ذَلِكَ أُمُّ سَلَمَةَ، فَأَرْسَلَ إِلَى أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ فَانْطَلَقَتْ مَعَ الرَّسُولِ فَسَأَلَ أُمَّ سَلَمَةَ فَقَالَتْ: يَرْحَمُهَا اللَّهُ مَا أَرَادَتْ إِلَى هَذَا، فَقَدْ أَخْبَرْتُهَا: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَهَى عَنْهُمَا، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بَيْنَمَا هُوَ فِي بَيْتِي يَتَوَضَّأُ الظُّهْرَ وَكَانَ قَدْ بَعَثَ سَاعِيًا وَكَثُرَ عِنْدَهُ الْمُهَاجِرُونَ، وَكَانَ قَدْ أَهَمَّهُ شَأْنُهُمْ، إِذْ ضَرَبَ الْبَابَ فَخَرَجَ إِلَيْهِ فَصَلَّى الظُّهْرَ، ثُمَّ جَلَسَ يُقِيمُ مَا جَاءَ بِهِ فَلَمْ يَزَلْ كَذَلِكَ حَتَّى صَلَّى الْعَصْرَ، فَلَمَّا فَرَغَ رَأَى بِلَالًا فَأَقَامَ الصَّلَاةَ فَصَلَّى الْعَصْرَ، دَخَلَ مَنْزِلِي فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، فَلَمَّا فَرَغَ قُلْتُ: مَا الرَّكْعَتَانِ رَأَيْتُكَ تُصَلِّيهِمَا بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ لَمْ أَرَكَ تُصَلِّيهِمَا؟ فَقَالَ: شَغَلَنِي أَمْرُ السَّاعِي لَمْ أَكُنْ صَلَّيْتُهُمَا بَعْدَ الظُّهْرِ فَصَلَّيْتُهَمَا فَقَالَ ابْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ: قَدْ صَلَّاهُمَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَأَنَا أُصَلِّيهِمَا

english.narrator: Ibn Idris → Yazid → Abdullah ibn al-Harith → Ibn Abbas → Mu’awiyah → Ibn az-Zubayr → Aishah → Umm Salamah
english.text: Ibn Idris narrated to us, from Yazid, from Abdullah ibn al-Harith, who said: I entered with Ibn Abbas upon Mu’awiyah, so Mu’awiyah seated him on the couch. Then he said to him: “What are these two Rak’ahs that people pray after Asr? We did not see the Messenger of Allah ﷺ pray them, nor did he command them.” He said: “That is what Ibn az-Zubayr instructs the people.” So he sent to Ibn az-Zubayr and asked him. He said: “Aishah informed me of that.” So he sent to Aishah, and she said: “Umm Salamah informed me of that.” So he sent to Umm Salamah, and she [or the messenger] went with the messenger. He asked Umm Salamah, and she said: “May Allah have mercy on her, she did not mean this. I had informed her that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade them. While the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was in my house performing ablution for Dhuhr, and he had sent a collector [for charity] and the Muhajirun had increased around him, and their affair concerned him, someone knocked on the door. He went out to him and prayed Dhuhr, then sat to distribute what had come. He continued like that until he prayed Asr. When he finished, he saw Bilal, so he called the Iqamah and prayed Asr. He entered my house and prayed two Rak’ahs. When he finished, I said: ‘What are these two Rak’ahs I saw you praying after Asr, which I have not seen you pray before?’ He said: ‘The affair of the collector busied me; I had not prayed them after Dhuhr, so I prayed them [now].’” Ibn az-Zubayr said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed them, so I pray them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا بُرْدَةَ بْنَ أَبِي مُوسَى يُصَلِّي بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Mu’adh ibn Mu’adh → Ibn Awn → Abu Burdah ibn Abi Musa
english.text: Mu’adh ibn Mu’adh narrated to us, from Ibn Awn, who said: “I saw Abu Burdah ibn Abi Musa praying two Rak’ahs after Asr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ بْنِ أَبِي الشَّعْثَاءِ قَالَ: خَرَجْتُ مَعَ أَبِي، وَعَمْرِو بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ، وَالْأَسْوَدِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، وَأَبِي وَائِلٍ فَكَانُوا يُصَلُّونَ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Dawud → Shu’bah → Ash’ath ibn Abi ash-Sha’tha → His Father & Amr ibn Maymun & Al-Aswad ibn Yazid & Abu Wa’il
english.text: Abu Dawud narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Ash’ath ibn Abi ash-Sha’tha, who said: “I went out with my father, Amr ibn Maymun, al-Aswad ibn Yazid, and Abu Wa’il, and they used to pray two Rak’ahs after Asr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: نا أَبُو عَوَانَةَ، قَالَ: ثنا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْمُنْتَشِرِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُصَلِّي بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، فَقِيلَ لَهُ: فَقَالَ: لَوْ لَمْ أُصَلِّهِمَا إِلَّا أَنِّي رَأَيْتُ مَسْرُوقًا يُصَلِّيهِمَا لَكَانَ ثِقَةً، وَلَكِنِّي سَأَلْتُ عَائِشَةَ فَقَالَتْ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَا يَدَعُ رَكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ الْفَجْرِ وَرَكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ

english.narrator: Affan → Abu Awanah → Ibrahim ibn Muhammad ibn al-Muntashir → His Father → Aishah
english.text: Affan narrated to us, saying: Abu Awanah narrated to us, saying: Ibrahim ibn Muhammad ibn al-Muntashir narrated to us, from his father, that he used to pray two Rak’ahs after Asr. It was said to him [about it], so he said: “If I only prayed them because I saw Masruq praying them, that would be trustworthy [enough]. But I asked Aishah, and she said: ‘The Messenger of Allah ﷺ never left two Rak’ahs before Fajr and two Rak’ahs after Asr.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: نا أَبُو عَوَانَةَ، قَالَ: نا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْمُنْتَشِرِ، عَنْ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، وَابْنِ شُرَيْحٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ شُرَيْحٌ يُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ أَخَذَهُمَا عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ

english.narrator: Affan → Abu Awanah → Ibrahim ibn Muhammad ibn al-Muntashir → Abu Talhah & Ibn Shurayh → Shurayh
english.text: Affan narrated to us, saying: Abu Awanah narrated to us, saying: Ibrahim ibn Muhammad ibn al-Muntashir narrated to us, from Abu Talhah and the son of Shurayh, who said: “Shurayh used to pray two Rak’ahs after Asr; he took them from Masruq.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: نا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّ الزُّبَيْرَ، وَعَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ، كَانَا يُصَلِّيَانِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Affan → Hammad ibn Salamah → Hisham ibn Urwah → His Father → Az-Zubayr & Abdullah ibn az-Zubayr
english.text: Affan narrated to us, saying: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Hisham ibn Urwah, from his father: “That az-Zubayr and Abdullah ibn az-Zubayr used to pray two Rak’ahs after Asr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ ضَمْرَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ: أَنَّهُ صَلَّى بِفُسْطَاطِهِ بِصِفِّينَ رَكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Isra’il → Abu Ishaq → Asim ibn Damrah → Ali
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Isra’il, from Abu Ishaq, from Asim ibn Damrah, from Ali: “That he prayed two Rak’ahs after Asr in his tent at Siffin.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا طَلْحَةُ بْنُ يَحْيَى، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عُبَيْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ، عَنْ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ، قَالَتْ: شُغِلَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنِ الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الظُّهْرِ فَصَلَّاهُمَا بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Talhah ibn Yahya → Ubayd Allah ibn Abdullah ibn Utbah → Umm Salamah
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Talhah ibn Yahya narrated to us, saying: I heard Ubayd Allah ibn Abdullah ibn Utbah, from Umm Salamah, who said: “The Prophet ﷺ was distracted from the two Rak’ahs after Dhuhr, so he prayed them after Asr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ حَبِيبِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الضُّحَى، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَتْنِي الصِّدِّيقَةُ بِنْتُ الصِّدِّيقِ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مَا دَخَلَ عَلَيْهَا بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ إِلَّا صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Ja’far ibn Awn → Mis’ar → Habib ibn Thabit → Abu ad-Duha → Masruq → As-Siddiqah bint as-Siddiq
english.text: Ja’far ibn Awn narrated to us, from Mis’ar, from Habib ibn Thabit, from Abu ad-Duha, from Masruq, who said: The truthful woman, daughter of the truthful man (Aishah), narrated to me: “That the Messenger of Allah ﷺ never entered upon her after Asr except that he prayed two Rak’ahs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ بْنُ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جُحَيْفَةَ عَنْهُمَا، قَالَ: إِنْ لَمْ تَنْفَعَاكَ فَلَمْ تَضُرَّاكَ

english.narrator: Abdul Wahhab ibn Ata → Shu’bah → Abu Ishaq → Abu Juhayfah
english.text: Abdul Wahhab ibn Ata narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Abu Ishaq, who said: I asked Abu Juhayfah about them [the two Rak’ahs after Asr]. He said: “If they do not benefit you, they will not harm you.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، وَأَبُو أُسَامَةَ، قَالَا: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: لَا تَتَحَيَّنَنَّ عِنْدَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ، وَلَا غُرُوبِهَا بِالصَّلَاةِ، فَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ كَانَ يَنْهَى عَنْ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abdullah ibn Numayr & Abu Usamah → Ubayd Allah ibn Umar → Nafi’ → Ibn Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abdullah ibn Numayr and Abu Usamah narrated to us, saying: Ubayd Allah ibn Umar narrated to us, from Nafi’, from Ibn Umar, who said: “Do not seek out the time of sunrise or sunset for prayer, for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to forbid that.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُوسَى بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عُقْبَةَ بْنِ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ "ثَلَاثُ سَاعَاتٍ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَنْهَانَا أَنْ نُصَلِّيَ فِيهَا، وَأَنْ نَقْبُرَ فِيهِنَّ مَوْتَانَا: حِينَ تَطْلُعُ الشَّمْسُ بَازِغَةً حَتَّى تَرْتَفِعَ، وَحِينَ تَضَيَّفُ لِلْغُرُوبِ حَتَّى تَغْرُبَ، وَحِينَ يَقُومُ قَائِمُ الظَّهِيرَةِ حَتَّى تَمِيلَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Musa ibn Ali → His Father → Uqbah ibn Amir
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Musa ibn Ali narrated to us, from his father, from Uqbah ibn Amir, who said: I heard [him say]: “Three times the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to forbid us from praying in or burying our dead: When the sun rises distinctly until it rises high, when it starts declining for sunset until it sets, and when the midday heat is at its peak [zenith] until it moves past [the meridian].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ زِرٍّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، قَالَ: "إِنَّ الشَّمْسَ تَطْلُعُ حِينَ تَطْلُعُ بَيْنَ قَرْنَيْ شَيْطَانٍ، قَالَ: فَكُنَّا نُنْهَى عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ عِنْدَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ وَعِنْدَ غُرُوبِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr ibn Ayyash → Asim → Zirr → Abdullah
english.text: Abu Bakr ibn Ayyash narrated to us, from Asim, from Zirr, from Abdullah, who said: “Indeed the sun rises, when it rises, between the horns of a devil.” He said: “So we were forbidden from praying at sunrise and at sunset.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ طَارِقِ بْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ بِلَالٍ، قَالَ: لَمْ يَنْهَ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ إِلَّا عِنْدَ غُرُوبِ الشَّمْسِ، لِأَنَّهَا تَغْرُبُ فِي قَرْنِ الشَّيْطَانِ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdi → Sufyan → Qays ibn Muslim → Tariq ibn Shihab → Bilal
english.text: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Qays ibn Muslim, from Tariq ibn Shihab, from Bilal, who said: “He did not forbid prayer except at sunset, because it sets in the horn of the devil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عُرْوَةُ بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ، أَنَّ أُنَاسًا طَافُوا بِالْبَيْتِ بَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ، ثُمَّ قَعَدُوا عِنْدَ الْمُذَكَّرِ، حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَ عِنْدَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ قَامُوا يُصَلُّونَ، قَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ: قَعَدُوا حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَتِ السَّاعَةُ الَّتِي يُكْرَهُ فِيهِ الصَّلَاةُ قَامُوا يُصَلُّونَ

english.narrator: Abdul Wahhab ath-Thaqafi → Habib → Ata → Urwah ibn az-Zubayr → Aishah
english.text: Abdul Wahhab ath-Thaqafi narrated to us, from Habib, from Ata, who said: Urwah ibn az-Zubayr narrated to me that some people performed Tawaf around the House after Fajr, then they sat by the place of remembrance, until when it was sunrise, they stood up to pray. Aishah said: “They sat until it was the hour in which prayer is disliked, then they stood up to pray.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ بِسْطَامِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي رَجَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: لَا تُصَلِّ عِنْدَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ وَلَا حِينَ تَغْرُبُ، فَإِنَّهَا تَطْلُعُ وَتَغْرُبُ فِي قَرْنَيْ شَيْطَانٍ، وَلَكِنْ إِذَا صَفَتْ وَعَلَتْ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Bistam ibn Muslim → Abu Raja’ → Ibn Abbas
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Bistam ibn Muslim, from Abu Raja’, from Ibn Abbas, who said: “Do not pray at sunrise nor when it sets, for it rises and sets between the horns of a devil, but [pray] when it becomes clear and high.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، عَنِ ابْنِ مُغَفَّلٍ، قَالَ: رَأَى أَبُو مَسْعُودٍ رَجُلًا يُصَلِّي عِنْدَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ أَوْ فِي السَّاعَةِ الَّتِي تُكْرَهُ فِيهَا الصَّلَاةُ، فَأَمَرَ رَجُلًا، فَنَهَاهُ

english.narrator: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn → Mis’ar → Ubayd → Al-Hasan → Ibn Mughaffal → Abu Mas’ud
english.text: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us, from Mis’ar, from Ubayd, from al-Hasan, from Ibn Mughaffal, who said: “Abu Mas’ud saw a man praying at sunrise or in the hour when prayer is disliked, so he ordered a man, and he forbade him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، أَنَّ شُرَيْحًا رَأَى رَجُلًا يُصَلِّي حِينَ اصْفَرَّتِ الشَّمْسُ، فَقَالَ: انْهَوْا هَذَا أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ، فَإِنَّ هَذِهِ سَاعَةٌ لَا تَحِلُّ فِيهَا الصَّلَاةُ

english.narrator: Abdul Wahhab → Ayyub → Muhammad → Shurayh
english.text: Abdul Wahhab narrated to us, from Ayyub, from Muhammad: That Shurayh saw a man praying when the sun had turned yellow [before sunset]. He said: “Forbid this one from praying, for this is an hour in which prayer is not lawful.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا هِشَامُ بْنُ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَا تَحَرَّوْا بِصَلَاتِكُمْ طُلُوعَ الشَّمْسِ وَلَا غُرُوبَهَا، فَإِنَّهَا تَطْلُعُ بِقَرْنِ الشَّيْطَانِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Hisham ibn Urwah → His Father → Ibn Umar
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Hisham ibn Urwah narrated to us, from his father, from Ibn Umar, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “Do not seek out with your prayer the rising of the sun nor its setting, for it rises with the horn of the devil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا هِشَامُ بْنُ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: إِذَا بَدَا حَاجِبُ الشَّمْسِ فَأَخِّرُوا الصَّلَاةَ حَتَّى تَبْرُزَ، وَإِذَا غَابَ حَاجِبُ الشَّمْسِ فَأَخِّرُوا الصَّلَاةَ حَتَّى تَغِيبَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Hisham ibn Urwah → His Father → Ibn Umar
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Hisham ibn Urwah narrated to us, from his father, from Ibn Umar, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “If the edge of the sun appears, then delay the prayer until it fully emerges; and if the edge of the sun disappears, then delay the prayer until it fully sets.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا عِيسَى بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ الرَّاسِبِيُّ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ الْحَسَنَ يَقُولُ: كَانُوا يَكْرَهُونَ الصَّلَاةَ عِنْدَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ حَتَّى تَرْتَفِعَ، وَعِنْدَ غُرُوبِهَا حَتَّى تَغِيبَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Isa ibn Humayd al-Rasibi → Al-Hasan
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Isa ibn Humayd al-Rasibi narrated to us, saying: I heard al-Hasan saying: “They used to dislike prayer at sunrise until it rose high, and at sunset until it disappeared.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ: مَا أُحِبُّ أَنَّ لِي بِصَلَاةِ الرَّجُلِ حِينَ تَطْلُعُ الشَّمْسُ فَلْسَيْنِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Hammad → Ibrahim → Ibn Mas’ud
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Hammad, from Ibrahim, [that] Ibn Mas’ud said: “I would not like to have two pennies [Fals] in exchange for a man’s prayer when the sun rises.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَفْرِيقِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَا صَلَاةَ بَعْدَ طُلُوعِ الْفَجْرِ إِلَّا رَكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Mu’awiyah → Al-Afriqi → Abdullah ibn Yazid → Abdullah ibn Amr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Mu’awiyah narrated to us, from al-Afriqi, from Abdullah ibn Yazid, from Abdullah ibn Amr, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “There is no prayer after the rising of Fajr except two Rak’ahs before the Fajr prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ الْيَمَانِيِّ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، وَابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَا: لَا صَلَاةَ بَعْدَ طُلُوعِ الْفَجْرِ، إِلَّا رَكْعَتَيِ الْفَجْرِ

english.narrator: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar → Hajjaj → Abu Muhammad al-Yamani → Tawus → Ibn Umar & Ibn Abbas
english.text: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar narrated to us, from Hajjaj, from Abu Muhammad al-Yamani, from Tawus, from Ibn Umar and Ibn Abbas, who both said: “There is no prayer after the rising of Fajr except the two Rak’ahs of Fajr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: لَا صَلَاةَ بَعْدَ رَكْعَتَيِ الْفَجْرِ حَتَّى تُصَلِّيَ الْفَجْرَ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Hajjaj → Nafi’ → Ibn Umar
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Hajjaj informed us, from Nafi’, from Ibn Umar that he said: “There is no prayer after the two Rak’ahs of Fajr until you pray the Fajr [obligatory prayer].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، قَالَ: رَآنِي سَعِيدُ بْنُ الْمُسَيِّبِ وَأَنَا أُصَلِّي بَعْضَ مَا فَاتَنِي مِنْ صَلَاةِ اللَّيْلِ بَعْدَمَا طَلَعَ الْفَجْرُ، فَقَالَ: أَمَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ الصَّلَاةَ تُكْرَهُ هَذِهِ السَّاعَةَ، إِلَّا رَكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ

english.narrator: Abu Mu’awiyah → Ash-Shaybani → Amr ibn Murrah → Sa’id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Abu Mu’awiyah narrated to us, from ash-Shaybani, from Amr ibn Murrah, who said: Sa’id ibn al-Musayyib saw me praying some of what I had missed of the night prayer after Fajr had risen. He said: “Did you not know that prayer is disliked at this hour, except for two Rak’ahs before the Fajr prayer?”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يَكْرَهُونَ إِذَا طَلَعَ الْفَجْرُ أَنْ يُصَلُّوا، إِلَّا رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Jarir → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Jarir narrated to us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: “They used to dislike praying when Fajr rose, except for two Rak’ahs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا مُغِيرَةُ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يَكْرَهُونَ الصَّلَاةَ بَعْدَ رَكْعَتَيِ الْفَجْرِ حَتَّى يُصَلُّوا الْمَكْتُوبَةَ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Mughirah informed us, from Ibrahim, who said: “They used to dislike prayer after the two Rak’ahs of Fajr until they prayed the obligatory prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: مَنْ شَاءَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ بَعْدَ طُلُوعِ الْفَجْرِ فَلْيَفْعَلْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abdah ibn Sulayman → Hisham ibn Urwah → His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abdah ibn Sulayman narrated to us, from Hisham ibn Urwah, from his father, who said: “Whoever wishes to pray after the rising of Fajr, let him do so.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: إِنَّ بَعْدَ طُلُوعِ الْفَجْرِ لَجُزْءًا حَسَنًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ، فَكَانَ يَقْرَأُ بَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ بِالْبَقَرَةِ

english.narrator: Hafs → Hisham ibn Urwah → His Father
english.text: Hafs narrated to us, from Hisham ibn Urwah, from his father, who said: “Indeed, after the rising of Fajr is a good portion of the night.” And he used to recite Al-Baqarah after Fajr.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ الْأَشَلِّ الْغُدَانِيِّ، قَالَ: سَأَلَ أَبُو حُصَيْنٍ الشَّعْبِيَّ، وَأَنَا أَسْمَعُ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ بَقِيَ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ وِرْدِهِ شَيْءٌ وَهُوَ يُصَلِّي وَقَدْ طَلَعَ الْفَجْرُ، فَقَالَ: يَقْرَأُ بَقِيَّةَ وِرْدِهِ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu’bah → Mansur ibn al-Ashall al-Ghudani → Abu Husayn → Ash-Sha’bi
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Mansur ibn al-Ashall al-Ghudani, who said: Abu Husayn asked ash-Sha’bi, while I was listening, about a man who had some of his Wird (daily portion of prayer/recitation) remaining, and he was praying while Fajr had risen. He said: “He recites the rest of his Wird.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا إِسْحَاقَ، وَالْحَكَمَ يُصَلِّيَانِ بَعْدَ طُلُوعِ الْفَجْرِ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu’bah → Abu Ishaq & Al-Hakam
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu’bah, who said: “I saw Abu Ishaq and al-Hakam praying after the rising of Fajr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ زِرٍّ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنَ عَوْفٍ، وَأُبَيَّ بْنَ كَعْبٍ إِذَا أَذَّنَ الْمُؤَذِّنُ لِلْمَغْرِبِ قَامَا يُصَلِّيَانِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sharik → Asim → Zirr → Abdul Rahman ibn Awf & Ubayy ibn Ka’b
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharik narrated to us, from Asim, from Zirr, who said: “I saw Abdul Rahman ibn Awf and Ubayy ibn Ka’b standing to pray two Rak’ahs when the Mu’adhin called the Adhan for Maghrib.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ عَنِ الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ الْمَغْرِبِ فَقَالَ: رَأَيْتُهُمْ إِذَا أَذَّنَ الْمُؤَذِّنُ ابْتَدَرُوا السَّوَارِيَ فَصَلَّوْا

english.narrator: Ath-Thaqafi → Humayd → Anas
english.text: Ath-Thaqafi narrated to us, from Humayd, from Anas, who said: He was asked about the two Rak’ahs before Maghrib. He said: “I saw them rushing to the pillars when the Mu’adhin called the Adhan, and they prayed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ أَبِي فَزَارَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَنَسًا عَنِ الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ الْمَغْرِبِ، فَقَالَ: كُنَّا نَبْتَدِرُهُمَا عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu’bah → Ya’la ibn Ata → Abu Fazarah → Anas
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from Ya’la ibn Ata, from Abu Fazarah, who said: I asked Anas about the two Rak’ahs before Maghrib. He said: “We used to rush to [perform] them during the time of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ أَبِي لَيْلَى صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ الْمَغْرِبِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Shu’bah → Al-Hakam → Ibn Abi Layla
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Shu’bah, from al-Hakam, who said: “I saw Ibn Abi Layla pray two Rak’ahs before Maghrib.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ حَبِيبِ بْنِ أَبِي ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، قَالَ: أَدْرَكْتُ أَصْحَابَ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُصَلُّونَ عِنْدَ كُلِّ تَأْذِينٍ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdi → Sufyan → Habib ibn Thabit → Mujahid → Ibn Abi Layla
english.text: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Habib ibn Thabit, from Mujahid, from Ibn Abi Layla, who said: “I encountered the companions of Muhammad ﷺ praying at every call to prayer [Adhan].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ كَهَمْسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ بُرَيْدَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْمُغَفَّلِ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: بَيْنَ كُلِّ آذَانَيْنِ صَلَاةٌ، بَيْنَ كُلِّ آذَانَيْنِ صَلَاةٌ، بَيْنَ كُلِّ آذَانَيْنِ صَلَاةٌ لِمَنْ شَاءَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Kahmas → Ibn Buraydah → Abdullah ibn al-Mughaffal
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Kahmas, from Ibn Buraydah, from Abdullah ibn al-Mughaffal, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “Between every two Adhans there is a prayer, between every two Adhans there is a prayer, between every two Adhans there is a prayer—for whoever wills.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنِ الْجُرَيْرِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ بُرَيْدَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْمُغَفَّلِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Abdul A’la → Al-Jurayri → Ibn Buraydah → Abdullah ibn al-Mughaffal
english.text: Abdul A’la narrated to us, from al-Jurayri, from Ibn Buraydah, from Abdullah ibn al-Mughaffal, from the Prophet ﷺ, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: قَالَ تَمِيمُ بْنُ سَلَّامٍ، أَوْ سَلَّامُ بْنُ تَمِيمَةَ لِلْحَسَنِ: مَا تَقُولُ فِي الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ الْمَغْرِبِ؟ فَقَالَ: حَسَنَتَانِ جَمِيلَتَانِ لِمَنْ أَرَادَ اللَّهُ بِهِمَا

english.narrator: Waki’ → Yazid ibn Ibrahim → Tamim ibn Salam → Al-Hasan
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Yazid ibn Ibrahim, who said: Tamim ibn Salam (or Salam ibn Tamim) said to al-Hasan: “What do you say about the two Rak’ahs before Maghrib?” He said: “Two good, beautiful [Rak’ahs] for whoever intends [to please] Allah with them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، قَالَ: مَا رَأَيْتُ فَقِيهًا يُصَلِّي قَبْلَ الْمَغْرِبِ إِلَّا سَعْدَ بْنَ أَبِي وَقَّاصٍ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Ibn Abi Arubah → Qatadah → Sa’id ibn al-Musayyib → Sa’d ibn Abi Waqqas
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Arubah, from Qatadah, from Sa’id ibn al-Musayyib, who said: “I never saw a Faqih (jurist) praying before Maghrib except Sa’d ibn Abi Waqqas.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ شَيْخًا، بِوَاسِطٍ يَقُولُ: سَمِعْتُ طَاوُسًا، سَأَلْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ عَنِ الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ الْمَغْرِبِ: فَلَمْ يَنْهَ عَنْهُمَا

english.narrator: Waki’ → Shu’bah → A Shaykh → Tawus → Ibn Umar
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Shu’bah, who said: I heard a shaykh in Wasit saying: I heard Tawus [say]: I asked Ibn Umar about the two Rak’ahs before Maghrib, and he did not forbid them.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ خَارِجَةَ بْنِ مُصْعَبٍ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ: لَا تَسْتَقْبِلْ الصُّورَةَ الصُّورَةَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Kharijah ibn Mus’ab → Zayd ibn Aslam → Ata ibn Yasar
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, from Kharijah ibn Mus’ab, from Zayd ibn Aslam, from Ata ibn Yasar, who said: The Messenger of Allah said: “Let not a face face a face [or an image face an image].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ قَالَ: كَانَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ جَالِسًا مُوَلِّيًا ظَهْرَهُ إِلَى الْقِبْلَةِ، وَإِنْسَانٌ قَائِمٌ يُصَلِّي مُسْتَقْبِلَهُ، فَأَخَذَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ يَتَّقِيهِ بِيَدِهِ مِنْ هَذَا الْجَانِبِ وَمِنْ هَذَا الْجَانِبِ

english.narrator: Abu Usamah → Ibn Awn → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Usamah narrated to us, from Ibn Awn, who said: “Ibrahim was sitting with his back to the Qiblah, and a person was standing praying facing him. So Ibrahim started shielding himself with his hand from this side and from this side.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَنْظَلَةُ الْجُمَحِيُّ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا نَذَرَ أَنْ يَسْجُدَ عَلَى جَبْهَةِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَأَتَمَّهُ، فَجَلَسَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مُسْتَقْبِلَ الْقِبْلَةِ، ثُمَّ أَقَامَ الرَّجُلُ خَلْفَهُ، وَقَالَ: هَكَذَا بِجَبْهَتِهِ فَسَجَدَ عَلَيْهَا

english.narrator: Waki’ → Hanzalah al-Jumahi → Tawus
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Hanzalah al-Jumahi narrated to us, from Tawus: That a man vowed to prostrate on the forehead of the Prophet ﷺ, and he [the Prophet] fulfilled it for him. The Prophet ﷺ sat facing the Qiblah, then the man stood behind him, and he did like this with his forehead, so he prostrated upon it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مَعْدَانَ بْنِ أَبِي مَعْدَانَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ طَاوُسًا، يُحَدِّثُ بِهَذَا الْحَدِيثِ، وَزَادَ فِيهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: قَدْ وَفَّيْتَ بِنَذْرِكَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Abdullah ibn Ma’dan ibn Abi Ma’dan → Tawus
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Abdullah ibn Ma’dan ibn Abi Ma’dan narrated to us, saying: I heard Tawus narrating this Hadith, and he added in it: The Prophet ﷺ said to him: “You have fulfilled your vow.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُهَرْوِلُ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mansur → Ibrahim → Al-Aswad
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, from al-Aswad: “That he used to hasten (jog) to prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنَ يَزِيدَ مُسَارِعًا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn Fudayl → Al-Hasan ibn Ubayd Allah → Ibrahim → Abdul Rahman ibn Yazid
english.text: Muhammad ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from al-Hasan ibn Ubayd Allah, from Ibrahim, who said: “I saw Abdul Rahman ibn Yazid hastening to prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، مِنْ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُهَرْوِلُ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Ibn Jurayj → A Man from Madinah → Ibn Umar
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Ibn Jurayj narrated to us, from a man from the people of Madinah, from Ibn Umar: “That he used to hasten (jog) to prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ: أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ الْإِقَامَةَ بِالْبَقِيعِ فَأَسْرَعَ الْمَشْيَ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Malik ibn Anas → Nafi’ → Ibn Umar
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Malik ibn Anas narrated to us, from Nafi’, from Ibn Umar: “That he heard the Iqamah at al-Baqi’, so he walked fast.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ يُهَرْوِلُ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sharik → Imran ibn Muslim → Sa’id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sharik narrated to us, from Imran ibn Muslim, who said: “I saw Sa’id ibn Jubayr hastening (jogging) to prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبِيدَةُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ يُهَرْوِلُ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Ubaydah ibn Humayd → Imran ibn Muslim → Sa’id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Ubaydah ibn Humayd narrated to us, from Imran ibn Muslim, who said: “I saw Sa’id ibn Jubayr hastening (jogging) to prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ قَيْسٍ الْمُلَائِيِّ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ كُهَيْلٍ، عَنْ عُمَارَةَ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: أَحَقُّ مَا سَعَيْنَا إِلَيْهِ الصَّلَاةُ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Amr ibn Qays al-Mula’i → Salamah ibn Kuhayl → Umarah ibn Umayr → Abdullah
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Amr ibn Qays al-Mula’i, from Salamah ibn Kuhayl, from Umarah ibn Umayr, who said: Abdullah said: “The most worthy thing we hasten to is prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ يُهَرْوِلُ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ فِي كُسُوفٍ وَمَعَهُ نَعْلَاهُ

english.narrator: Waki’ → Sufyan → Asim ibn Ubayd Allah → Ibn Umar
english.text: Waki’ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us, from Asim ibn Ubayd Allah, who said: “I saw Ibn Umar hastening (jogging) to the mosque during an eclipse, carrying his shoes.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ رِوَايَةً قَالَ: إِذَا أَتَيْتُمُ الصَّلَاةَ فَلَا تَأْتُوهَا وَأَنْتُمْ تَسْعَوْنَ، وَأْتُوهَا وَعَلَيْكُمُ السَّكِينَةُ، فَمَا أَدْرَكْتُمْ فَصَلُّوا وَمَا فَاتَكُمْ فَاقْضُوا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Uyaynah → Az-Zuhri → Sa’id ibn al-Musayyib → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Uyaynah narrated to us, from az-Zuhri, from Sa’id ibn al-Musayyib, from Abu Hurayrah (as a narration from the Prophet): “If you come to prayer, do not come to it running. Come to it with tranquility (Sakinah). Whatever you catch, pray; and whatever you missed, make up.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: إِذَا أَتَيْتُمُ الصَّلَاةَ فَأْتُوهَا بِالْوَقَارِ وَالسَّكِينَةِ، فَمَا أَدْرَكْتُمْ فَصَلُّوا، وَمَا فَاتَكُمْ فَأَتِمُّوا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Sa‘d ibn Ibrāhīm → ‘Umar ibn Salamah → Abū Hurayrah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, he said: Sufyān narrated to us, from Sa‘d ibn Ibrāhīm, from ‘Umar ibn Salamah, from Abū Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “When you come to the prayer, come to it with dignity and calmness. Whatever you catch [of the prayer], pray it, and whatever you missed, complete it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ أَبِي نَضْرَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ، قَالَ: إِذَا أُقِيمَتِ الصَّلَاةُ فَامْشِ إِلَيْهَا كَمَا كُنْتَ تَمْشِي، فَصَلِّ مَا أَدْرَكْتَ وَاقْضِ مَا سَبَقَكَ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyūb → ‘Amr → Abū Naḍrah → Abū Dharr
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ayyūb, from ‘Amr, from Abū Naḍrah, from Abū Dharr, who said: “When the prayer is established, walk to it just as you usually walk. Then pray whatever you catch, and make up whatever you missed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: إِذَا ثُوِّبَ بِالصَّلَاةِ فَامْشُوا وَعَلَيْكُمُ السَّكِينَةَ وَالْوَقَارَ، فَصَلُّوا مَا أَدْرَكْتُمْ وَاقْضُوا مَا سَبَقَكُمْ

english.narrator: Al-Thaqafī → Ayyūb → Muḥammad → Abū Hurayrah
english.text: Al-Thaqafī narrated to us, from Ayyūb, from Muḥammad, from Abū Hurayrah, who said: “When the call for prayer is made (Iqāmah), walk [to it] and adhere to calmness and dignity. Pray whatever you catch, and make up whatever you missed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: امْشُوا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ، وَقَارِبُوا بَيْنَ الْخُطَا، وَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Abū Isḥāq → Abū ‘Ubaydah → ‘Abdullāh
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, he said: Sufyān narrated to us, from Abū Isḥāq, from Abū ‘Ubaydah, from ‘Abdullāh [ibn Mas‘ūd], who said: “Walk to the prayer, keep your steps short (close together), and remember Allah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا الْمَسْعُودِيُّ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْأَقْمَرِ، عَنْ أَبِي الْأَحْوَصِ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: لَقَدْ رَأَيْتُنَا وَإِنَّا لَنُقَارِبُ بَيْنَ الْخُطَا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Al-Mas‘ūdī → ‘Alī ibn al-Aqmar → Abū al-Aḥwaṣ → ‘Abdullāh
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, he said: Al-Mas‘ūdī narrated to us, from ‘Alī ibn al-Aqmar, from Abū al-Aḥwaṣ, who said: ‘Abdullāh [ibn Mas‘ūd] said: “I have seen us [the companions] keeping our steps short (close together) while going to the prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ حَيَّانَ أَبُو الْأَشْهَبِ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ الْبُنَانِيِّ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: خَرَجْتُ مَعَ زَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ فَأَسْرَعْتُ الْمَشْيَ فَحَبَسَنِي

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ja‘far ibn Ḥayyān Abū al-Ashhab → Thābit al-Bunānī → Anas ibn Mālik
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, he said: Ja‘far ibn Ḥayyān Abū al-Ashhab narrated to us, from Thābit al-Bunānī, from Anas ibn Mālik, who said: “I went out with Zayd ibn Thābit to the mosque. I walked quickly, so he restrained me.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ فَرَاهِيجَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي مَوْلَايَ سُفْيَانُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَنْطَلِقُ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ وَهُوَ يَسْتَعْجِلُ، قَالَ: فَلَحِقَنِي الزُّبَيْرُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ فَقَالَ: اقْصِدْ فِي مَشْيِكَ، فَإِنَّكَ فِي صَلَاةٍ، لَنْ تَخْطُوَ خُطْوَةً إِلَّا رَفَعَ اللَّهُ لَكَ بِهَا دَرَجَةً وَحَطَّ عَنْكَ بِهَا خَطِيئَةً

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu‘bah → Dāwūd ibn Farāhīj → Sufyān ibn Ziyād → Al-Zubayr ibn al-‘Awwām
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from Dāwūd ibn Farāhīj, who said: My master Sufyān ibn Ziyād narrated to me that he used to go to the mosque hurrying. He said: “Al-Zubayr ibn al-‘Awwām caught up with me and said: ‘Be moderate in your walking, for indeed you are in prayer. You will not take a step except that Allah raises you a degree by it and removes a sin from you by it.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنْ خَالِدِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، عَنْ بُهَيَّةَ حَاضِنَةِ بَنِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، قَالَتْ: سَمِعْتُ الْإِقَامَةَ فَأَسْرَعْتُ، فَمَرَرْتُ بِعَلِيِّ بْنِ حُسَيْنٍ وَأَنَا مُسْرِعَةٌ فَجَذَبَ ثَوْبِي، وَقَالَ: امْشِ عَلَى رِسْلِكَ

english.narrator: Ma‘n ibn ‘Īsā → Khālid ibn Abī Bakr → Buhayyah → ‘Alī ibn Ḥusayn
english.text: Ma‘n ibn ‘Īsā narrated to us, from Khālid ibn Abī Bakr, from Buhayyah, the nursemaid of Banū ‘Abdullāh, she said: “I heard the Iqāmah (call to commence prayer) so I hurried. I passed by ‘Alī ibn Ḥusayn while I was rushing, so he pulled my garment and said: ‘Walk slowly/at your ease.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا يَحْيَى بْنُ يَعْلَى، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، قَالَ: عُدْنَا مُجَاهِدًا، أَنَا وَصَاحِبٌ لِي، فَحَضَرَتِ الصَّلَاةُ، فَقَالَ: انْطَلِقُوا فَصَلُّوا، وَامْشُوا عَلَى هَيْنَتِكُمْ، فَمَا أَدْرَكْتُمْ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ فَصَلُّوا، وَمَا فَاتَكُمْ فَأَتِمُّوا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Yaḥyā ibn Ya‘lā → ‘Uthmān ibn al-Aswad → Mujāhid
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, he said: Yaḥyā ibn Ya‘lā narrated to us, from ‘Uthmān ibn al-Aswad, who said: “My companion and I visited Mujāhid (when he was sick). The time for prayer arrived, so he said: ‘Go and pray, and walk gently/at your ease. Whatever you catch with the Imam, pray it, and whatever you missed, complete it.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ أَبِي سِنَانٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ خُلَيْدَةَ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ أَمْشِي مَعَ ابْنِ عُمَرَ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ، فَلَوْ مَشَتْ مَعَهُ نَمْلَةٌ لَرَأَيْتُ أَنْ لَا يَسْبِقَهَا

english.narrator: Ibn Fuḍayl → Abū Sinān → Muḥammad ibn Zayd ibn Khulaydah → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from Abū Sinān, from Muḥammad ibn Zayd ibn Khulaydah, who said: “I was walking with Ibn ‘Umar to the prayer. If an ant were to walk with him, I thought he would not outpace it (meaning he walked very slowly).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: أنا حُمَيْدٌ الطَّوِيلُ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ، قَالَ: أَخَذَ بِيَدِي أَنَسٌ فَجَعَلَ يَمْشِي رُوَيْدًا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ، ثُمَّ الْتَفَتَ إِلَيَّ فَقَالَ: هَكَذَا كَانَ يَصْنَعُ زَيْدُ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ لِيُكْثِرَ خُطَاهُ

english.narrator: Yazīd ibn Hārūn → Ḥumayd al-Ṭawīl → Thābit → Anas → Zayd ibn Thābit
english.text: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, he said: Ḥumayd al-Ṭawīl informed us, from Thābit, who said: “Anas took me by the hand and began walking slowly to the prayer. Then he turned to me and said: ‘This is what Zayd ibn Thābit used to do in order to increase his steps.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ ثَابِتِ بْنِ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: قَالَ لِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: نَاوِلِينِي الْخُمْرَةَ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ، فَقُلْتُ: إِنِّي حَائِضٌ، قَالَ: إِنَّ حَيْضَتَكِ لَيْسَتْ فِي يَدِكِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Mu'awiyah → Al-A'mash → Thabit ibn Ubayd → Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad → Aishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Mu'awiyah narrated to us, from Al-A'mash, from Thabit ibn Ubayd, from Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad, from Aishah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to me: "Hand me the Khumrah (small prayer mat) from the mosque." I said: "I am menstruating." He said: "Indeed, your menstruation is not in your hand."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ عَنِ الْحَائِضِ تُنَاوِلُ الطَّهُورَ أَوِ الشَّيْءَ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ، فَقَالَ: إِنَّ حَيْضَتَهَا لَيْسَتْ فِي يَدِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Usamah → Hisham → Al-Hasan → Ibn Umar
english.text: Abu Usamah narrated to us, from Hisham, from Al-Hasan, who said: Ibn Umar was asked about a menstruating woman handing over purification water or something from the mosque. He said: "Indeed, her menstruation is not in her hand."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ تَضَعَ الْحَائِضُ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الشَّيْءَ وَتَأْخُذَهُ مِنْهُ وَلَا تَدْخُلُهُ

english.narrator: Jarir → Mansur → Ibrahim
english.text: Jarir narrated to us, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, who said: "There is no harm if a menstruating woman puts something in the mosque or takes it from it, but she should not enter it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا أَنْ تَضَعَ الْحَائِضُ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ مَا شَاءَتْ وَتَأْخُذَهُ مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abdul-A'la → Ma'mar → Az-Zuhri
english.text: Abdul-A'la narrated to us, from Ma'mar, from Az-Zuhri: "That he saw no harm in a menstruating woman putting whatever she wished in the mosque or taking it from it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ الدَّسْتُوَائِيِّ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، قَالَ: الْحَائِضُ تَأْخُذُ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ، وَلَا تَضَعُ فِيهِ

english.narrator: Ibn Ulayyah → Hisham Ad-Dastawa'i → Qatadah
english.text: Ibn Ulayyah narrated to us, from Hisham Ad-Dastawa'i, from Qatadah, who said: "A menstruating woman may take from the mosque, but she should not put anything in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْلَى بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ، عَنْ جَدَّتِهِ الرَّبَابِ، أَنَّ عُثْمَانَ بْنَ حُنَيْفٍ قَالَ: يَا جَارِيَةُ نَاوِلِينِي الْخُمْرَةَ، قَالَتْ: لَسْتُ أُصَلِّي، قَالَ: إِنَّ حَيْضَتَكِ لَيْسَتْ فِي يَدِكِ

english.narrator: Ya'la ibn Ubayd → Uthman ibn Hakim → His Grandmother Ar-Rabab → Uthman ibn Hunayf
english.text: Ya'la ibn Ubayd narrated to us, from Uthman ibn Hakim, from his grandmother Ar-Rabab, that Uthman ibn Hunayf said: "O servant girl, hand me the Khumrah." She said: "I am not praying (i.e., menstruating)." He said: "Indeed, your menstruation is not in your hand."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ لِجَارِيَتِهِ: نَاوِلِينِي الْخُمْرَةَ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ، فَتَقُولُ: إِنِّي حَائِضٌ، فَيَقُولُ: إِنَّ حَيْضَتَكِ لَيْسَتْ فِي يَدِكِ

english.narrator: Ibn Numayr → Ubayd Allah ibn Umar → Nafi' → Ibn Umar
english.text: Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Ubayd Allah ibn Umar, from Nafi', from Ibn Umar, that he used to say to his servant girl: "Hand me the Khumrah from the mosque." She would say: "I am menstruating." He would say: "Indeed, your menstruation is not in your hand."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ الدَّسْتُوَائِيِّ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: تَأْخُذُ الْحَائِضُ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ وَتَضَعُ فِيهِ

english.narrator: Ibn Ulayyah → Hisham Ad-Dastawa'i → Hammad → Ibrahim
english.text: Ibn Ulayyah narrated to us, from Hisham Ad-Dastawa'i, from Hammad, from Ibrahim, who said: "A menstruating woman may take from the mosque and put things in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْلَى بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ فِي الْحَائِضِ تُنَاوِلُ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الشَّيْءَ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ، إِلَّا الْمُصْحَفَ

english.narrator: Ya'la ibn Ubayd → Abdul-Malik → Ata
english.text: Ya'la ibn Ubayd narrated to us, from Abdul-Malik, from Ata, regarding a menstruating woman handing something into the mosque? He said: "Yes, except the Mushaf (Quran)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا أَنْ تَأْخُذَ الْحَائِضُ الشَّيْءَ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ وَتَضَعَهُ فِيهِ

english.narrator: Ibn Abi Adi → Ash'ath → Al-Hasan
english.text: Ibn Abi Adi narrated to us, from Ash'ath, from Al-Hasan: "That he saw no harm in a menstruating woman taking something from the mosque or putting it in."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَبُو وَائِلٍ يُرْسِلُ خَادِمَتَهُ وَهِيَ حَائِضٌ إِلَى أَبِي رَزِينٍ، فَتَأْتِيهِ بِالْمُصْحَفِ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ، فَتُمْسِكُ بِعِلَاقَتِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mughirah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us, from Mughirah, who said: "Abu Wa'il used to send his servant girl while she was menstruating to Abu Razin. She would bring him the Mushaf from him, holding it by its strap."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَتَنَاوَلَ الرَّجُلُ الْمُصْحَفَ إِذَا كَانَ فِي وِعَائِهِ أَوْ فِي عِلَاقَتِهِ

english.narrator: Hafs → Ash'ath → Al-Hasan
english.text: Hafs narrated to us, from Ash'ath, from Al-Hasan, who said: "There is no harm if a man (without wudu) takes the Mushaf if it is in its case or by its strap."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ بْنُ حَرْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِي خَالِدٍ الدَّالَانِيِّ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ يَعْنِي الْأَعْرَجَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ قَرَأَ فِي الْمُصْحَفِ، ثُمَّ نَاوَلَ غُلَامًا لَهُ مَجُوسِيًّا بِعِلَاقَتِهِ

english.narrator: Abdus-Salam ibn Harb → Abu Khalid Ad-Dalani → Al-Qasim (Al-A'raj)
english.text: Abdus-Salam ibn Harb narrated to us, from Abu Khalid Ad-Dalani, from Al-Qasim (meaning Al-A'raj), who said: "I saw Sa'id ibn Jubayr read from the Mushaf, then he handed it to a Magian servant of his by its strap."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَيْمَنَ بْنِ نَابِلٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عَطَاءً يَقُولُ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ تَأْخُذَ الْحَائِضُ بِعِلَاقَةِ الْمُصْحَفِ

english.narrator: Waki' → Ayman ibn Nabil → Ata
english.text: Waki' narrated to us, from Ayman ibn Nabil, who said: I heard Ata say: "There is no harm if a menstruating woman holds the strap of the Mushaf."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ: أَنَّهُ لَمْ يَكُنْ يَرَى بَأْسًا أَنْ يَحْمِلَ الرَّجُلُ الْمُصْحَفَ وَهُوَ غَيْرُ طَاهِرٍ

english.narrator: Abu Usamah → Hisham → Muhammad
english.text: Abu Usamah narrated to us, from Hisham, from Muhammad: "That he did not see any harm in a man carrying the Mushaf while he is not pure."
